1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""I-arch-007 BREADTH REPLAY HARNESS - the behavioral acceptance gate for the
3	consolidation fix.
4	
5	WHAT THIS PROVES (and what it does NOT)
6	---------------------------------------
7	The forensic finding (state/iarch007_breadth_collapse_finding.md) is: the rendered
8	report cites 7 of 787 visible sources and shows "Multi-source corroborated (>=2
9	origins): 0". The runtime log says ``[finding-dedup] raw=787 distinct=99
10	collapsed=0 -> 787 generator rows``. Diff-review kept missing the bug because every
11	phase was "green + flag-set + Codex-approved" while consolidation silently no-op'd
12	in the OUTPUT. This harness replays the REAL consolidation code on a banked corpus
13	snapshot and FAILS LOUD (non-zero exit) if consolidation is still a no-op.
14	
15	THE INVERTED ASSERTION (read before editing)
16	--------------------------------------------
17	``collapsed>0`` is the WRONG acceptance signal. Under ``PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN``
18	(default-ON), ``dedup_by_finding`` KEEPS ALL ROWS (CONSOLIDATE-keep-all, CLAUDE.md
19	§-1.3 Principle 2), so ``collapsed_row_count == 0`` BY DESIGN - the code comment
20	literally says "collapsed_row_count honestly becomes 0". A harness asserting
21	``collapsed>0`` would fail on a correctly-consolidated corpus and pass only when the
22	legacy DROP path runs (the bug). So this harness asserts the REAL consolidation
23	signal instead:
24	
25	    >>> at least one finding cluster has corroboration_count >= 2
26	
27	``corroboration_count`` = ``count_independent_hosts`` over registrable domains - a
28	genuine multi-origin signal, computed with NO LLM. It also asserts the redesign flag
29	is ON and ``collapsed == 0`` (proving keep-all engaged, so a regression to the legacy
30	drop path ALSO fails the gate).
31	
32	FAITHFULNESS HARD CONSTRAINT
33	----------------------------
34	Breadth must come from CONSOLIDATING already-verified corroborators into multi-citation
35	baskets, NEVER from relaxing a gate. This harness touches NO faithfulness gate. It
36	calls the SAME pure ``dedup_by_finding`` the live run calls
37	(``run_honest_sweep_r3.py:8079``) with the SAME flag state and SAME corpus rows. The
38	fuller mode's per-member verify is the REAL ``verify_sentence_provenance`` (an LLM
39	entailment call) - it is never faked, because a fake verify_fn would change what passes
40	verification (BANNED).
41	
42	THE THREE NUMBERS (keep them distinct - the checkpoints file warns of exactly this)
43	-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
44	1. STRUCTURAL pre-verify corroboration (Tier-1, no LLM): clusters with
45	   ``corroboration_count >= 2``. This is the consolidation CEILING - necessary, NOT
46	   sufficient. Tier-1 passing does NOT prove breadth fired in the output.
47	2. VERIFIED multi-origin baskets (``verified_support_origin_count >= 2``): needs the
48	   real ``verify_fn`` (LLM). The number that reaches the report's "Multi-source
49	   corroborated" line.
50	3. DISTINCT-CITED in the rendered report (``#ev`` tokens / bibliography): the actual
51	   "fired in the output." Only a full render produces this - fuller mode approximates
52	   the ceiling, it does not re-render.
53	
54	Usage
55	-----
56	    # Tier-1 (no LLM) - the hard acceptance gate every consolidation fix MUST pass:
57	    python scripts/breadth_replay_harness.py \
58	        --corpus outputs/corpus_backups/drb_75_metal_ions_cvd_corpus.tgz \
59	        --domain clinical --expect-rows 787
60	
61	    # or point at an already-extracted snapshot dir / file:
62	    python scripts/breadth_replay_harness.py \
63	        --corpus outputs/clean_deepseek/clinical/drb_75_metal_ions_cvd/corpus_snapshot.json \
64	        --domain clinical
65	
66	    # Fuller mode - also build the claim graph + baskets (the --baskets verify is the
67	    # ONE unavoidable LLM call; --no-verify reports the structural ceiling only):
68	    python scripts/breadth_replay_harness.py --corpus <...> --domain clinical --baskets --no-verify
69	
70	Exit codes: 0 = consolidation produced >=1 multi-origin basket (gate PASS).
71	            2 = consolidation is a NO-OP (gate FAIL - loud).
72	            3 = could not load / replay (corpus missing, schema mismatch, etc.).
73	
74	Pure-ish: constructs no network client in Tier-1. Fuller-mode --baskets --verify is the
75	only path that issues LLM calls, and only through the production verifier.
76	"""
77	from __future__ import annotations
78	
79	import argparse
80	import json
81	import os
82	import sys
83	import tarfile
84	import tempfile
85	from collections import Counter
86	from pathlib import Path
87	from typing import Any
88	
89	# Project root on sys.path so ``src.polaris_graph...`` imports resolve when run as a script.
90	_REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
91	if str(_REPO_ROOT) not in sys.path:
92	    sys.path.insert(0, str(_REPO_ROOT))
93	
94	SNAPSHOT_FILENAME = "corpus_snapshot.json"
95	
96	
97	def _eprint(*a: Any) -> None:
98	    # ASCII-safe on a Windows cp1252 console (box-drawing glyphs would raise).
99	    msg = " ".join(str(x) for x in a)
100	    try:
101	        print(msg, file=sys.stderr, flush=True)
102	    except UnicodeEncodeError:
103	        print(msg.encode("ascii", "replace").decode("ascii"), file=sys.stderr, flush=True)
104	
105	
106	def _load_snapshot_rows(corpus: str) -> tuple[list[dict], dict]:
107	    """Load ``evidence_for_gen`` from a .tgz, a snapshot dir, or a snapshot .json.
108	
109	    Returns (rows, payload). Fails loud (CorpusReplayError) on a missing/empty/
110	    version-mismatched snapshot - never silently substitutes an empty corpus.
111	    """
112	    p = Path(corpus)
113	    snap_path: Path | None = None
114	    if p.is_file() and p.suffix in (".tgz", ".gz") or str(p).endswith(".tar.gz"):
115	        tmp = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix="breadth_replay_"))
116	        with tarfile.open(p) as tf:
117	            member = next((m for m in tf.getmembers()
118	                           if Path(m.name).name == SNAPSHOT_FILENAME), None)
119	            if member is None:
120	                raise CorpusReplayError(f"{p}: no {SNAPSHOT_FILENAME} inside the archive")
121	            tf.extract(member, tmp)  # noqa: S202 - trusted local backup, data-only JSON
122	            snap_path = tmp / member.name
123	    elif p.is_dir():
124	        snap_path = p / SNAPSHOT_FILENAME
125	    elif p.is_file():
126	        snap_path = p
127	    if snap_path is None or not snap_path.exists():
128	        raise CorpusReplayError(f"{corpus}: could not locate {SNAPSHOT_FILENAME}")
129	
130	    payload = json.loads(snap_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
131	    if not isinstance(payload, dict):
132	        raise CorpusReplayError(f"{snap_path}: snapshot is not a JSON object")
133	    rows = payload.get("evidence_for_gen") or []
134	    if not rows:
135	        raise CorpusReplayError(
136	            f"{snap_path}: empty evidence_for_gen - refusing to replay an empty corpus"
137	        )
138	    return list(rows), payload
139	
140	
141	class CorpusReplayError(RuntimeError):
142	    """Fail-loud: the banked corpus could not be replayed (LAW II, no silent green)."""
143	
144	
145	# Step-4 gate (task interface): only when PG_BASKET_CONSUME_FINDING_DEDUP is SET do we
146	# run credibility_pass's basket assembly. Mirrors credibility_pass off-value semantics.
147	_BASKET_STEP_FLAG = "PG_BASKET_CONSUME_FINDING_DEDUP"
148	_OFF_VALUES = {"", "0", "off", "false", "no"}
149	
150	
151	def _basket_step_requested() -> bool:
152	    """True iff PG_BASKET_CONSUME_FINDING_DEDUP is explicitly SET to a non-off value."""
153	    raw = os.environ.get(_BASKET_STEP_FLAG, "")
154	    return raw != "" and raw.strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
155	
156	
157	def _resolve_corpus_for_slug(slug: str) -> str:
158	    """Resolve a slug to a corpus path: prefer an already-extracted
159	    ``outputs/clean_local/<slug>/corpus_snapshot.json``; else fall back to
160	    ``outputs/corpus_backups/<slug>_corpus.tgz``. FAIL LOUD if neither exists.
161	
162	    NOTE (cQ75): the clean_local dir may exist WITHOUT corpus_snapshot.json (it holds
163	    only bibliography/report/run_status/verification_details), so we check for the
164	    snapshot FILE, not just the dir, before falling through to the tgz.
165	    """
166	    snap = _REPO_ROOT / "outputs" / "clean_local" / slug / SNAPSHOT_FILENAME
167	    if snap.is_file():
168	        return str(snap)
169	    tgz = _REPO_ROOT / "outputs" / "corpus_backups" / f"{slug}_corpus.tgz"
170	    if tgz.is_file():
171	        return str(tgz)
172	    raise CorpusReplayError(
173	        f"slug {slug!r}: no snapshot at {snap} and no backup at {tgz}. Cannot replay."
174	    )
175	
176	
177	def run_tier1(rows: list[dict], domain: str, expect_rows: int | None) -> dict[str, Any]:
178	    """Replay the REAL ``dedup_by_finding`` exactly as run_honest_sweep_r3.py:8079 does.
179	
180	    Returns a report dict. Raises on a replay/import failure so the caller exits 3.
181	    """
182	    # Import here so a bad environment surfaces as exit-3 (replay error), not a stack at top.
183	    from src.polaris_graph.authority.data_loader import load_authority_data
184	    from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.credibility_pass import credibility_redesign_enabled
185	    from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.finding_dedup import dedup_by_finding
186	
187	    redesign_on = credibility_redesign_enabled()
188	    gov = load_authority_data()["psl_gov_suffixes"]
189	
190	    # EXACT live call shape (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:8079-8082).
191	    res = dedup_by_finding(rows, gov_suffixes=gov, domain=domain)
192	
193	    clusters = res.clusters
194	    multi = [c for c in clusters if c.corroboration_count >= 2]
195	    # A REAL basket is a multi-MEMBER cluster on a KNOWN subject (the unknown-subject
196	    # sentinel can never merge, so a corrob>=2 on a ``__unknown__`` key is a single row
197	    # with two hosts, NOT a consolidated basket - report it separately, honestly).
198	    real_baskets = [
199	        c for c in clusters
200	        if c.corroboration_count >= 2
201	        and len(c.member_indices) >= 2
202	        and not (isinstance(c.finding_key, tuple)
203	                 and len(c.finding_key) >= 1 and c.finding_key[0] == "__unknown__")
204	    ]
205	    # Honest disclosure: a corroboration>=2 cluster on an ``__unknown__`` finding_key is
206	    # NOT genuine cross-row consolidation — the unknown-subject sentinel embeds the row's
207	    # evidence_id, so two rows only "merge" onto it when they SHARE an evidence_id (a data
208	    # quirk: duplicate evidence_id across distinct rows). Surface these so corr>=2 is never
209	    # mistaken for breadth when it is a sentinel artifact (the cQ75 case: ev_713 dup).
210	    sentinel_corr_clusters = [
211	        {
212	            "finding_key": list(c.finding_key)[:3],
213	            "member_indices": list(c.member_indices),
214	            "shared_evidence_ids": sorted({
215	                str(rows[ri].get("evidence_id", ri)) for ri in c.member_indices
216	            }),
217	            "hosts": list(c.member_hosts[:5]),
218	        }
219	        for c in clusters
220	        if c.corroboration_count >= 2 and len(c.member_indices) >= 2
221	        and isinstance(c.finding_key, tuple) and c.finding_key and c.finding_key[0] == "__unknown__"
222	    ]
223	    corr_dist = dict(sorted(Counter(c.corroboration_count for c in clusters).items()))
224	    member_dist = dict(sorted(Counter(len(c.member_indices) for c in clusters).items()))
225	    distinct_cited_ceiling = len({
226	        str(r.get("evidence_id", i)) for i, r in enumerate(res.deduped_rows)
227	    })
228	
229	    report = {
230	        "tier": 1,
231	        "redesign_flag_on": redesign_on,
232	        "raw_row_count": res.raw_row_count,
233	        "distinct_finding_count": res.distinct_finding_count,
234	        "collapsed_row_count": res.collapsed_row_count,
235	        "deduped_rows": len(res.deduped_rows),
236	        "clusters_total": len(clusters),
237	        "clusters_corroboration_ge2": len(multi),
238	        "real_multi_member_baskets": len(real_baskets),
239	        "max_corroboration": max((c.corroboration_count for c in clusters), default=0),
240	        "sentinel_corroboration_artifacts": sentinel_corr_clusters,
241	        "corroboration_distribution": corr_dist,
242	        "member_count_distribution": member_dist,
243	        "distinct_cited_ceiling": distinct_cited_ceiling,
244	        "expect_rows": expect_rows,
245	        "input_fidelity_ok": (expect_rows is None or res.raw_row_count == expect_rows),
246	        "top_baskets": [
247	            {
248	                "corroboration_count": c.corroboration_count,
249	                "members": len(c.member_indices),
250	                "hosts": list(c.member_hosts[:5]),
251	                "finding_key": list(c.finding_key)[:4],
252	            }
253	            for c in sorted(real_baskets or multi,
254	                            key=lambda c: c.corroboration_count, reverse=True)[:5]
255	        ],
256	    }
257	    return report
258	
259	
260	def evaluate_gate(report: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple[bool, list[str]]:
261	    """The acceptance assertions. Returns (passed, failure_reasons).
262	
263	    HARD assertions (any failure => gate FAIL, exit 2):
264	      A1. redesign flag is ON           (keep-all path is the one under test)
265	      A2. collapsed_row_count == 0       (proves keep-all engaged; a >0 means the
266	                                          legacy DROP path regressed back in)
267	      A3. clusters_corroboration_ge2 > 0 (CONSOLIDATION IS NOT A NO-OP - the real signal)
268	      A4. real_multi_member_baskets > 0  (>=1 KNOWN-subject multi-member basket - the
269	                                          unknown-subject sentinel does not count)
270	      A5. input_fidelity_ok              (raw_row_count matches --expect-rows if given,
271	                                          proving we replayed the SAME corpus)
272	    """
273	    reasons: list[str] = []
274	    if not report["redesign_flag_on"]:
275	        reasons.append(
276	            "A1 FAIL: PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN is OFF - the legacy filter-and-cap "
277	            "drop path is active; this harness gates the WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE path. "
278	            "Set PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN=on (the Gate-B / live default)."
279	        )
280	    if report["collapsed_row_count"] != 0:
281	        reasons.append(
282	            f"A2 FAIL: collapsed_row_count={report['collapsed_row_count']} != 0 - the "
283	            "legacy collapse-to-representative DROP re-engaged (CONSOLIDATE-keep-all "
284	            "regressed). Under the redesign flag every same-claim row must flow through."
285	        )
286	    if report["clusters_corroboration_ge2"] <= 0:
287	        reasons.append(
288	            "A3 FAIL: ZERO clusters with corroboration_count>=2 - consolidation is a "
289	            "NO-OP. finding_dedup detected "
290	            f"{report['distinct_finding_count']} distinct findings among "
291	            f"{report['raw_row_count']} rows but grouped NONE into a multi-source "
292	            "basket. This is the exact breadth-collapse bug (Multi-source corroborated=0)."
293	        )
294	    if report["real_multi_member_baskets"] <= 0:
295	        reasons.append(
296	            "A4 FAIL: ZERO known-subject multi-member baskets - every corroboration>=2 "
297	            "cluster (if any) is an unknown-subject sentinel (a single row with multiple "
298	            "hosts, which CANNOT merge across rows by the conservative-singleton rule), "
299	            "NOT a consolidated basket. The extractor returns 'unknown'/nothing for the "
300	            "rows, so real consolidation never happens."
301	        )
302	    if not report["input_fidelity_ok"]:
303	        reasons.append(
304	            f"A5 FAIL: raw_row_count={report['raw_row_count']} != --expect-rows="
305	            f"{report['expect_rows']} - replayed a DIFFERENT corpus than the live run saw."
306	        )
307	    return (not reasons), reasons
308	
309	
310	def run_tier2_baskets(rows: list[dict], domain: str, *, verify: bool) -> dict[str, Any]:
311	    """Fuller mode: build the claim graph + assemble baskets on the snapshot.
312	
313	    ATTRIBUTION (precise): the GROUPING/consolidation half is FULLY OFFLINE and
314	    deterministic. ``build_claim_graph(nli_judge=None)`` is EXACTLY production's call
315	    shape (credibility_pass.py:653 calls it with no nli_judge), so the multi-member
316	    cluster count this reports is the SAME number production gets — genuine sparsity on
317	    this corpus, NOT an offline limitation. Only the per-member VERIFY
318	    (``verify_sentence_provenance`` -> the NLI entailment judge) is LLM-bearing, and it
319	    feeds ONLY ``verified_support_origin_count`` (the report's 'Multi-source corroborated
320	    (>=2 verified origins)' number) — never the grouping.
321	
322	    With ``verify=False`` the basket STRUCTURE (claim clusters x members) is reported as
323	    the offline ceiling, NO LLM call. ``verified_support_origin_count`` requires
324	    ``--verify`` (the ONE unavoidable real entailment call). The harness NEVER substitutes
325	    a fake verify_fn and NEVER sets PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT=off — either would change
326	    what passes verification (BANNED, CLAUDE.md §-1.3).
327	    """
328	    from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.claim_graph import build_claim_graph
329	
330	    # nli_judge=None == production's call shape (credibility_pass.py:653) — offline,
331	    # deterministic; the count here equals production's, never an offline downgrade.
332	    graph = build_claim_graph(rows, domain=domain, nli_judge=None)
333	    clusters = getattr(graph, "clusters", {}) or {}
334	    claims = getattr(graph, "claims", []) or []
335	    multi_member_clusters = [cid for cid, idxs in clusters.items() if len(idxs) >= 2]
336	
337	    out: dict[str, Any] = {
338	        "tier": 2,
339	        "claim_count": len(claims),
340	        "claim_clusters_total": len(clusters),
341	        "claim_clusters_multi_member": len(multi_member_clusters),
342	        "verify_ran": False,
343	        "note": (
344	            "Structural basket ceiling (no LLM). verified_support_origin_count needs the "
345	            "real verify_fn; pass --verify to run it (the ONE unavoidable LLM call)."
346	        ),
347	    }
348	
349	    if verify:
350	        import os as _os
351	        from src.polaris_graph.authority.data_loader import load_authority_data
352	        from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.credibility_pass import run_credibility_analysis
353	        # run_credibility_analysis wires the production verify_sentence_provenance internally
354	        # (the SAME path the live run uses). Its per-member isolated verify is the REAL NLI
355	        # entailment gate — an LLM call — so this branch is LLM/spend-bearing (guarded by
356	        # --verify). judge=None ⇒ no separate credibility judge (priors-only, disclosed gap),
357	        # but the entailment verify still fires; we NEVER inject a fake verify_fn (BANNED:
358	        # a deterministic stand-in would change what passes verification). Serial isolated
359	        # verify for determinism.
360	        _os.environ.setdefault("PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_MAX_INFLIGHT", "1")
361	        gov = tuple(load_authority_data()["psl_gov_suffixes"])
362	        result = run_credibility_analysis(
363	            "breadth_replay_harness basket assembly", rows,
364	            gov_suffixes=gov, domain=domain, judge=None,
365	        )
366	        baskets = getattr(result, "baskets", None) or getattr(result, "claim_baskets", []) or []
367	        verified_multi = [
368	            b for b in baskets
369	            if int(getattr(b, "verified_support_origin_count", 0) or 0) >= 2
370	        ]
371	        out.update({
372	            "verify_ran": True,
373	            "baskets_total": len(baskets),
374	            "verified_multi_origin_baskets": len(verified_multi),
375	            "note": (
376	                "verified_multi_origin_baskets == the report's 'Multi-source corroborated "
377	                "(>=2 origins)' number. The rendered distinct-cited count (#ev tokens) is "
378	                "produced only by a full render - fuller mode reports the ceiling."
379	            ),
380	        })
381	    return out
382	
383	
384	def run_tier_k_render(rows: list[dict], *, k_min: int) -> dict[str, Any]:
385	    """FIX K acceptance gate — does the verified-span render flip 0->many citations?
386	
387	    Replays the EXACT production K path on the snapshot:
388	      build_verified_span_draft(ev_ids, pool) -> _rewrite_draft_with_spans -> strict_verify
389	    and counts the DISTINCT sources whose own verbatim span survives the UNCHANGED gate.
390	    The bug it guards: the enrichment section rendered EMPTY (590 unbound-SUPPORTS in -> 0
391	    cited) because the LLM re-generated prose that re-failed strict_verify. K skips the LLM
392	    and surfaces each source's already-verified span; this gate FAILS LOUD if the rendered
393	    distinct-cited count does not clear ``k_min`` (i.e. K still collapses).
394	
395	    Faithfulness: NO gate is relaxed. The entailment strictness is whatever
396	    ``PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT`` is in the environment — run with ``=off`` for the
397	    DETERMINISTIC floor (offline, no LLM), or unset (=enforce, the production gate) for the
398	    real number (LLM-bearing). This harness NEVER fakes the gate. The ev_id set here is the
399	    full usable-quote pool (the offline ceiling proxy for the live unbound-SUPPORTS
400	    selection); the real selection is a subset, so a PASS here is necessary, not inflated.
401	    """
402	    import os as _os
403	
404	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.live_deepseek_generator import (
405	        _rewrite_draft_with_spans,
406	    )
407	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import (
408	        parse_provenance_tokens,
409	        strict_verify,
410	    )
411	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.weighted_enrichment import (
412	        build_verified_span_draft,
413	        spans_per_source,
414	    )
415	
416	    evidence_pool = {
417	        str(r.get("evidence_id")): r for r in rows if r.get("evidence_id")
418	    }
419	    ev_ids = [
420	        str(r.get("evidence_id"))
421	        for r in rows
422	        if r.get("evidence_id") and (r.get("direct_quote") or "").strip()
423	    ]
424	    draft = build_verified_span_draft(ev_ids, evidence_pool)
425	    rewritten, converted, unverifiable = _rewrite_draft_with_spans(draft, evidence_pool)
426	    report = strict_verify(rewritten, evidence_pool)
427	
428	    cited: set[str] = set()
429	    for sv in report.kept_sentences:
430	        toks = getattr(sv, "tokens", None) or parse_provenance_tokens(
431	            getattr(sv, "sentence", "") or ""
432	        )
433	        for tok in toks:
434	            eid = getattr(tok, "evidence_id", None)
435	            if eid:
436	                cited.add(str(eid))
437	
438	    entail_mode = _os.environ.get("PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT", "enforce")
439	    return {
440	        "tier": "k_render",
441	        "entailment_mode": entail_mode,
442	        "spans_per_source": spans_per_source(),
443	        "ev_ids_offered": len(ev_ids),
444	        "draft_chars": len(draft),
445	        "markers_rewritten": converted,
446	        "markers_unverifiable": unverifiable,
447	        "sentences_kept": report.total_kept,
448	        "sentences_in": report.total_in,
449	        "distinct_cited_sources": len(cited),
450	        "k_min": k_min,
451	        "passed": len(cited) >= k_min,
452	    }
453	
454	
455	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
456	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__,
457	                                 formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter)
458	    ap.add_argument("--slug", default="drb_75_metal_ions_cvd",
459	                    help="corpus slug (default drb_75_metal_ions_cvd). Resolved to "
460	                         "outputs/clean_local/<slug>/corpus_snapshot.json if present, else "
461	                         "outputs/corpus_backups/<slug>_corpus.tgz. Overridden by --corpus.")
462	    ap.add_argument("--corpus", default=None,
463	                    help="explicit path to a *_corpus.tgz, a run dir, or a corpus_snapshot.json "
464	                         "(overrides --slug)")
465	    ap.add_argument("--domain", default=None,
466	                    help="run-level domain to pin the extractor (e.g. clinical); "
467	                         "defaults to the snapshot's own 'domain' field")
468	    ap.add_argument("--expect-rows", type=int, default=None,
469	                    help="assert raw_row_count == this (input-fidelity guard)")
470	    ap.add_argument("--baskets", action="store_true",
471	                    help="fuller mode: also build the claim graph + baskets")
472	    ap.add_argument("--verify", dest="verify", action="store_true",
473	                    help="(with --baskets) run the REAL per-member verify (LLM/spend-bearing)")
474	    ap.add_argument("--no-verify", dest="verify", action="store_false",
475	                    help="(with --baskets) structural ceiling only, no LLM (default)")
476	    ap.set_defaults(verify=False)
477	    ap.add_argument("--k-render", action="store_true",
478	                    help="FIX K gate: replay build_verified_span_draft -> rewrite -> "
479	                         "strict_verify and assert distinct-cited >= --k-min. Run with "
480	                         "PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT=off for the deterministic floor.")
481	    ap.add_argument("--k-min", type=int, default=50,
482	                    help="FIX K gate floor: minimum distinct-cited sources to PASS (default 50; "
483	                         "the broken baseline is ~7).")
484	    ap.add_argument("--json", action="store_true", help="emit the report as JSON to stdout")
485	    args = ap.parse_args(argv)
486	
487	    # Resolve corpus: explicit --corpus wins; else resolve the --slug.
488	    try:
489	        corpus = args.corpus or _resolve_corpus_for_slug(args.slug)
490	    except CorpusReplayError as exc:
491	        _eprint(f"[breadth-replay] REPLAY ERROR: {exc}")
492	        return 3
493	
494	    try:
495	        rows, payload = _load_snapshot_rows(corpus)
496	    except (CorpusReplayError, OSError, json.JSONDecodeError) as exc:
497	        _eprint(f"[breadth-replay] REPLAY ERROR: {exc}")
498	        return 3
499	    domain = args.domain or payload.get("domain")
500	
501	    # Task interface: PG_BASKET_CONSUME_FINDING_DEDUP set ⇒ also run the basket step
502	    # (equivalent to --baskets). Honest constraint: the per-member verify is the REAL
503	    # NLI entailment gate (an LLM call), so a faithful basket verdict is NOT no-LLM.
504	    # We therefore run the STRUCTURAL basket ceiling (no LLM) by default and only issue
505	    # the verify LLM call when --verify is passed. verified_support_origin_count (the
506	    # task's >=2 assertion) requires that real verify — never a fake verify_fn (BANNED).
507	    run_baskets = args.baskets or _basket_step_requested()
508	
509	    # FIX K gate: a SEPARATE acceptance gate (verified-span render), not the consolidation
510	    # one. Run it standalone so its PASS/FAIL is unambiguous and exit code reflects only K.
511	    if args.k_render:
512	        try:
513	            krep = run_tier_k_render(rows, k_min=args.k_min)
514	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - any replay failure is exit-3, fail loud
515	            _eprint(f"[breadth-replay] REPLAY ERROR (FIX-K execution): {exc!r}")
516	            return 3
517	        if args.json:
518	            print(json.dumps(krep, indent=2, default=str))
519	        else:
520	            _eprint("== BREADTH REPLAY (FIX-K verified-span render gate) ==")
521	            _eprint(f"  corpus              : {corpus}")
522	            _eprint(f"  entailment_mode     : {krep['entailment_mode']} "
523	                    f"(off=deterministic floor; enforce=production gate, LLM-bearing)")
524	            _eprint(f"  spans_per_source    : {krep['spans_per_source']}")
525	            _eprint(f"  ev_ids offered      : {krep['ev_ids_offered']}")
526	            _eprint(f"  sentences kept / in : {krep['sentences_kept']} / {krep['sentences_in']}")
527	            _eprint(f"  DISTINCT CITED      : {krep['distinct_cited_sources']} "
528	                    f"(broken baseline ~7; k_min={krep['k_min']})")
529	            if krep["passed"]:
530	                _eprint("  VERDICT             : PASS - K surfaced the verified-span basket.")
531	            else:
532	                _eprint("  VERDICT             : FAIL - K still collapses (distinct-cited "
533	                        "below k_min). The render is NOT firing in the output.")
534	        return 0 if krep["passed"] else 1
535	
536	    try:
537	        report = run_tier1(rows, domain=domain, expect_rows=args.expect_rows)
538	        if run_baskets:
539	            report["tier2"] = run_tier2_baskets(rows, domain=domain, verify=args.verify)
540	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - any replay failure is exit-3, fail loud
541	        _eprint(f"[breadth-replay] REPLAY ERROR (Tier-1/2 execution): {exc!r}")
542	        return 3
543	
544	    passed, reasons = evaluate_gate(report)
545	
546	    if args.json:
547	        print(json.dumps({"report": report, "passed": passed, "reasons": reasons},
548	                         indent=2, default=str))
549	    else:
550	        _eprint("== BREADTH REPLAY (consolidation acceptance gate) ==")
551	        _eprint(f"  corpus            : {corpus}")
552	        _eprint(f"  domain            : {domain}")
553	        _eprint(f"  redesign_flag_on  : {report['redesign_flag_on']}")
554	        _eprint(f"  raw / distinct    : {report['raw_row_count']} / "
555	                f"{report['distinct_finding_count']}")
556	        _eprint(f"  collapsed         : {report['collapsed_row_count']} (must be 0 = keep-all)")
557	        _eprint(f"  clusters corr>=2  : {report['clusters_corroboration_ge2']}")
558	        _eprint(f"  REAL baskets      : {report['real_multi_member_baskets']} "
559	                f"(known-subject, >=2 members)")
560	        _eprint(f"  max corroboration : {report['max_corroboration']}")
561	        if report.get("sentinel_corroboration_artifacts"):
562	            _eprint(f"  SENTINEL ARTIFACTS: {len(report['sentinel_corroboration_artifacts'])} "
563	                    f"corr>=2 cluster(s) are unknown-subject sentinels (dup evidence_id, NOT breadth):")
564	            for s in report["sentinel_corroboration_artifacts"]:
565	                _eprint(f"    - shared_evidence_ids={s['shared_evidence_ids']} "
566	                        f"rows={s['member_indices']} hosts={s['hosts']}")
567	        _eprint(f"  corr distribution : {report['corroboration_distribution']}")
568	        _eprint(f"  cited ceiling     : {report['distinct_cited_ceiling']}")
569	        if report.get("tier2"):
570	            t2 = report["tier2"]
571	            _eprint(f"  [tier2] claim clusters multi-member: "
572	                    f"{t2['claim_clusters_multi_member']}/{t2['claim_clusters_total']}")
573	            if t2.get("verify_ran"):
574	                _eprint(f"  [tier2] VERIFIED multi-origin baskets: "
575	                        f"{t2['verified_multi_origin_baskets']}/{t2['baskets_total']}")
576	        if passed:
577	            _eprint("  VERDICT           : PASS - consolidation produced >=1 multi-origin basket.")
578	        else:
579	            _eprint("  VERDICT           : FAIL - consolidation is a NO-OP:")
580	            for r in reasons:
581	                _eprint(f"    - {r}")
582	
583	    return 0 if passed else 2
584	
585	
586	if __name__ == "__main__":
587	    raise SystemExit(main())
588

Let me check for identifier-level flags: no unprofessional identifiers found in code (variable/function names are descriptive). Continuing.

1	"""Build the REAL canonical demo bundle from a real pipeline run.
2	
3	I-p2-036 (#795): the prior canonical demo bundle had PLACEHOLDER source text
4	(claim not in source = fabricated proof). This rebuilds it from a REAL run
5	(real claims + real source spans that genuinely contain them). Maps the run's
6	verified sentences (verification_details.json: sentence + token{evidence_id,
7	start,end}) + the real fetched source text (evidence_pool.json: direct_quote)
8	into the v1.0 signed-bundle format the inspector loads.
9	
10	§-1.1 gate: a sentence is featured ONLY if every numeric token in it appears in
11	the cited span (strict_verify-style numeric-fidelity re-check). Spans that fail
12	are dropped — we never ship a claim whose source span doesn't support it.
13	
14	Signature: NONE (operator chose "skip the seal" — option b). No manifest.yaml.asc
15	is written, so the inspector honestly renders signaturePresent=false.
16	"""
17	
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	
20	import hashlib
21	import json
22	import re
23	import sys
24	from pathlib import Path
25	
26	RUN = Path("outputs/honest_sweep_r3/clinical/clinical_tirzepatide_t2dm")
27	OUT = Path("web/public/canonical_bundles/v1_canonical_success")
28	DECISION_ID = "decision_real_tirzepatide_0001"
29	POOL_ID = "pool_real_tirzepatide_0001"
30	REPORT_ID = "report_real_tirzepatide_0001"
31	BUNDLE_ID = "bundle_real_tirzepatide_0001"
32	
33	_NUM = re.compile(r"\d+(?:\.\d+)?")
34	_PROV_TOKEN = re.compile(r"\[#ev:[^\]]*\]")
35	
36	
37	def strip_tokens(s: str) -> str:
38	    """Remove inline provenance tokens [#ev:id:start-end] — they are metadata,
39	    NOT claim content. (Their id/offset digits would otherwise pollute the
40	    numeric-fidelity check and the displayed claim text.)"""
41	    return _PROV_TOKEN.sub("", s).strip()
42	
43	
44	def _norm(s: str) -> str:
45	    # normalise unicode minus/hyphen variants so numeric matching is fair.
46	    return s.replace("−", "-").replace("‐", "-").replace("–", "-")
47	
48	
49	def _tier_counts(sources: list[dict]) -> dict:
50	    counts: dict[str, int] = {}
51	    for s in sources:
52	        t = s.get("tier", "T1")
53	        counts[t] = counts.get(t, 0) + 1
54	    return dict(sorted(counts.items()))
55	
56	
57	def numbers_present(sentence: str, span: str) -> tuple[bool, list[str]]:
58	    """Every numeric token in the sentence must appear in the span (§-1.1)."""
59	    nums = _NUM.findall(_norm(sentence))
60	    span_n = _norm(span)
61	    missing = [n for n in nums if n not in span_n]
62	    return (len(missing) == 0, missing)
63	
64	
65	def main() -> int:
66	    vd = json.loads((RUN / "verification_details.json").read_text("utf-8"))
67	    pool = json.loads((RUN / "evidence_pool.json").read_text("utf-8"))
68	    protocol = json.loads((RUN / "protocol.json").read_text("utf-8"))
69	    by_id = {s["evidence_id"]: s for s in pool}
70	
71	    sections = []
72	    cited_ids: set[str] = set()
73	    total_kept = 0
74	    total_dropped_gate = 0
75	
76	    for sec in vd["sections"]:
77	        title = sec.get("title", "Findings")
78	        vsents = []
79	        for k in sec.get("kept") or []:
80	            sentence = strip_tokens(k["sentence"])
81	            toks = k.get("tokens") or []
82	            if not toks:
83	                continue
84	            prov_tokens = []
85	            ok_any = False
86	            for tok in toks:
87	                eid = tok["evidence_id"]
88	                start, end = tok["start"], tok["end"]
89	                ev = by_id.get(eid)
90	                if not ev:
91	                    continue
92	                dq = ev.get("direct_quote", "") or ""
93	                span = dq[start:end]
94	                if not span.strip():
95	                    continue
96	                ok, missing = numbers_present(sentence, span)
97	                if not ok:
98	                    print(
99	                        f"  [§-1.1 DROP] {eid}:{start}-{end} missing nums "
100	                        f"{missing} :: {sentence[:70]!r}"
101	                    )
102	                    continue
103	                prov_tokens.append(f"[#ev:{eid}:{start}-{end}]")
104	                cited_ids.add(eid)
105	                ok_any = True
106	            if not ok_any:
107	                total_dropped_gate += 1
108	                continue
109	            vsents.append(
110	                {
111	                    "section_id": title,
112	                    "sentence_text": sentence,
113	                    "provenance_tokens": prov_tokens,
114	                    "verifier_pass": True,
115	                    "assertion_surface": "prose",
116	                    "contradiction": None,
117	                    "drop_reason": None,
118	                    "evaluator_agrees": True,
119	                    "evaluator_disagreement": None,
120	                    "is_synthesis_claim": False,
121	                }
122	            )
123	            total_kept += 1
124	        if vsents:
125	            sections.append(
126	                {
127	                    "section_id": title,
128	                    "section_title": title,
129	                    "section_status": "verified",
130	                    "section_verify_pass_rate": 1.0,
131	                    "verified_sentences": vsents,
132	                }
133	            )
134	
135	    if total_kept == 0:
136	        print("FATAL: no sentences passed the §-1.1 gate", file=sys.stderr)
137	        return 1
138	
139	    question = protocol.get("question") or protocol.get("research_question") or ""
140	
141	    verified_report = {
142	        "report_id": REPORT_ID,
143	        "pool_id": POOL_ID,
144	        "decision_id": DECISION_ID,
145	        "sections": sections,
146	        "overall_verify_pass_rate": 1.0,
147	        "verifier_pass_threshold": 0.4,
148	        "pipeline_verdict": "success",
149	        "generator_model": "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro",
150	        "evaluator_model": "google/gemma-4-31b-it",
151	        "family_segregation_passed": True,
152	        "started_at_utc": "2026-05-20T00:00:00+00:00",
153	        "finished_at_utc": "2026-05-20T00:00:00+00:00",
154	        "latency_ms": 0,
155	        "cost_usd": 0.0,
156	        "research_question": question,
157	    }
158	
159	    sources = []
160	    for eid in sorted(cited_ids):
161	        ev = by_id[eid]
162	        url = ev.get("source_url", "") or ""
163	        domain = url.split("/")[2] if "://" in url else (ev.get("source") or "")
164	        sources.append(
165	            {
166	                "source_id": eid,
167	                # rstrip trailing whitespace per line so git diff --check is clean
168	                "full_text": "\n".join(
169	                    line.rstrip() for line in (ev.get("direct_quote", "") or "").split("\n")
170	                ),
171	                "full_text_available": True,
172	                "title": (ev.get("statement") or ev.get("title") or eid)[:200],
173	                "url": url,
174	                "domain": domain,
175	                "tier": ev.get("tier", "T1"),
176	                "authors": [],
177	                "snippet": (ev.get("direct_quote", "") or "")[:200],
178	                "retracted": False,
179	                "fetched_at_utc": "2026-05-20T00:00:00+00:00",
180	                "provenance": {"fetched_by": "live_retriever"},
181	            }
182	        )
183	
184	    evidence_pool = {
185	        "pool_id": POOL_ID,
186	        "decision_id": DECISION_ID,
187	        "sources": sources,
188	        "adequacy": {
189	            "is_adequate": True,
190	            "failure_reason": None,
191	            "min_required_per_tier": {"T1": 1},
192	            # honest per-tier counts from the actual source tiers (not all-T1)
193	            "sources_per_tier": _tier_counts(sources),
194	        },
195	        "queries_executed": [question] if question else [],
196	        "cost_usd": 0.0,
197	        "latency_ms": 0,
198	        "retrieval_started_at_utc": "2026-05-20T00:00:00+00:00",
199	        "retrieval_finished_at_utc": "2026-05-20T00:00:00+00:00",
200	    }
201	
202	    scope_decision = {
203	        "decision_id": DECISION_ID,
204	        "scope_class": "clinical_efficacy",
205	        "status": "in_scope",
206	        "ambiguity_axes": [],
207	        "clarifications_needed": [],
208	        "decided_at_utc": "2026-05-20T00:00:00+00:00",
209	        "latency_ms": 0,
210	        "provenance": {"classifier_layer": "llm", "ambiguity_detector_layer": "llm"},
211	        "research_question": question,
212	    }
213	
214	    metadata = {
215	        "bundle_created_at_utc": "2026-05-20T00:00:00+00:00",
216	        "generator_model": "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro",
217	        "evaluator_model": "google/gemma-4-31b-it",
218	        "polaris_version": "1.0.0",
219	        "schema_version": "1.0",
220	        "research_question": question,
221	        "source": "real pipeline run: honest_sweep_r3 clinical_tirzepatide_t2dm",
222	    }
223	
224	    reasoning_trace = [
225	        {
226	            "call_id": "call_0001",
227	            "call_type": "verify",
228	            "section": "verification",
229	            "status": "ok",
230	            "content_source": "real_run",
231	            "content_text": (
232	                f"{total_kept} sentences span-verified against real sources; "
233	                f"{total_dropped_gate} dropped at the §-1.1 numeric gate."
234	            ),
235	            "model": "google/gemma-4-31b-it",
236	            "timestamp": "2026-05-20T00:00:00+00:00",
237	        }
238	    ]
239	
240	    # --- write files ---
241	    if OUT.exists():
242	        for p in OUT.rglob("*"):
243	            if p.is_file():
244	                p.unlink()
245	    (OUT / "sources").mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
246	
247	    def write(path: Path, data: str) -> None:
248	        path.write_text(data, encoding="utf-8")
249	
250	    write(OUT / "scope_decision.json", json.dumps(scope_decision, indent=2))
251	    write(OUT / "evidence_pool.json", json.dumps(evidence_pool, indent=2))
252	    write(OUT / "verified_report.json", json.dumps(verified_report, indent=2))
253	    write(OUT / "metadata.json", json.dumps(metadata, indent=2))
254	    with (OUT / "reasoning_trace.jsonl").open("w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
255	        for rec in reasoning_trace:
256	            f.write(json.dumps(rec) + "\n")
257	    for src in sources:
258	        write(OUT / "sources" / f"{src['source_id']}.txt", src["full_text"])
259	
260	    # --- manifest (sha256 + sizes; NO .asc → signature honestly absent) ---
261	    file_entries = [
262	        ("scope_decision", "scope_decision.json"),
263	        ("evidence_pool", "evidence_pool.json"),
264	        ("verified_report", "verified_report.json"),
265	        ("metadata", "metadata.json"),
266	        ("reasoning_trace", "reasoning_trace.jsonl"),
267	    ]
268	    for src in sources:
269	        file_entries.append(("source_snapshot", f"sources/{src['source_id']}.txt"))
270	
271	    files = []
272	    for ctype, rel in file_entries:
273	        b = (OUT / rel).read_bytes()
274	        files.append(
275	            {
276	                "content_type": ctype,
277	                "path": rel,
278	                "sha256": hashlib.sha256(b).hexdigest(),
279	                "size_bytes": len(b),
280	            }
281	        )
282	
283	    manifest_lines = [
284	        "bundle_created_at_utc: '2026-05-20T00:00:00Z'",
285	        f"bundle_id: {BUNDLE_ID}",
286	        "bundle_version: '1.0'",
287	        f"decision_id: {DECISION_ID}",
288	        "files:",
289	    ]
290	    for fe in files:
291	        manifest_lines += [
292	            f"- content_type: {fe['content_type']}",
293	            f"  path: {fe['path']}",
294	            f"  sha256: {fe['sha256']}",
295	            f"  size_bytes: {fe['size_bytes']}",
296	        ]
297	    manifest_lines += [
298	        "generator_model: deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro",
299	        "polaris_version: 1.0.0",
300	        f"pool_id: {POOL_ID}",
301	        f"report_id: {REPORT_ID}",
302	    ]
303	    write(OUT / "manifest.yaml", "\n".join(manifest_lines) + "\n")
304	
305	    print(
306	        f"OK: {total_kept} verified sentences across {len(sections)} sections, "
307	        f"{len(sources)} real sources. {total_dropped_gate} dropped at §-1.1 gate. "
308	        f"No signature (option b)."
309	    )
310	    return 0
311	
312	
313	if __name__ == "__main__":
314	    raise SystemExit(main())
315

content.text: Clean — no unprofessional identifiers, the code uses "canonical" and "real" as data descriptors, not marketing adjectives per se (they're describing "real data vs placeholder" distinction which is a legitimate domain concept). Continuing.

content.content:
1	"""Build the S2 clinical fixture (GH #983, Phase 0a smoke S2).
2	
3	Reconstructs FULL ``ClassificationSignals`` for every unique clinical URL in the
4	frozen ``outputs/honest_sweep_r3/clinical/**/live_corpus_dump.json`` corpus,
5	plus the additive ``AuthoritySignals`` payload, plus the HEAD output tier — and
6	freezes them to ``tests/fixtures/authority/clinical_200_urls.jsonl``.
7	
8	OFFLINE + NON-LAUNDERING (honors brief §6 caveat):
9	  The signals are recovered from the dump's recorded ``tier_rule`` +
10	  ``tier_reasons`` — which deterministically encode the exact OpenAlex
11	  publication_type / source_type / is_peer_reviewed / fetched_content_length
12	  HEAD used. This is NOT a url+title re-derivation (which the brief forbids):
13	  it reads back the actual classifier-input signals HEAD captured in its own
14	  audit trail. No network call is made.
15	
16	DEGRADATION NOTE (operator + Codex): the brief assumed ~200 clinical URLs and a
17	one-time live OpenAlex re-fetch. The frozen offline corpus contains 40 unique
18	clinical URLs and the task forbids any live API spend / network. So the fixture
19	freezes ALL real clinical URLs available offline (the honest maximum) with
20	signals recovered from the audit trail rather than a re-fetch. No synthetic
21	sources are added (LAW II).
22	"""
23	from __future__ import annotations
24	
25	import glob
26	import json
27	import re
28	import sys
29	from pathlib import Path
30	
31	REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
32	if str(REPO_ROOT) not in sys.path:
33	    sys.path.insert(0, str(REPO_ROOT))
34	
35	CLINICAL_GLOB = str(
36	    REPO_ROOT / "outputs" / "honest_sweep_r3" / "clinical" / "**" / "live_corpus_dump.json"
37	)
38	OUT_PATH = REPO_ROOT / "tests" / "fixtures" / "authority" / "clinical_200_urls.jsonl"
39	
40	_STUB_RE = re.compile(r"Fetched body is (\d+) chars")
41	
42	
43	def _recover_signals(row: dict) -> dict:
44	    """Recover the OpenAlex input signals HEAD used, from rule + reasons.
45	
46	    These are the ACTUAL classifier-input signals HEAD recorded in its audit
47	    trail (rule id + reason text), not a url+title re-derivation. The mapping
48	    is deterministic per rule id.
49	    """
50	    rule = row.get("tier_rule", "") or ""
51	    reason = " ".join(row.get("tier_reasons") or [])
52	    title = row.get("title", "") or ""
53	    pub_type = ""
54	    src_type = ""
55	    is_peer = False
56	    content_len = 5000  # default: a normally-fetched (non-stub) body
57	
58	    # Content length recovered from the stub reason when present.
59	    m = _STUB_RE.search(reason)
60	    if m:
61	        content_len = int(m.group(1))
62	
63	    # R1 stub + R6 abstract-only-domain are stub-content semantics: HEAD treated
64	    # the body as too thin for full classification (T7). Recover as stub so the
65	    # view's stub_content_t7 rule reproduces T7 (these are NOT scholarly tiers).
66	    if rule == "R6_abstract_only_domain":
67	        content_len = 1   # below the 1000-char stub threshold
68	
69	    if rule.startswith("R9_") or rule.startswith("R11_"):
70	        # OpenAlex-driven rules carry pub_type / source_type in the reason.
71	        # 'review' check first so "narrative review" doesn't false-match below.
72	        if "'review'" in reason or "publication_type == 'review'" in reason:
73	            pub_type = "review"
74	        elif "'article'" in reason:
75	            pub_type = "article"
76	        elif "'editorial'" in reason:
77	            pub_type = "editorial"
78	        elif "'letter'" in reason:
79	            pub_type = "letter"
80	        elif "preprint" in reason:
81	            pub_type = "article"
82	        if "in journal" in reason or "'journal'" in reason:
83	            src_type = "journal"
84	        elif "repository" in reason or "'repository'" in reason:
85	            src_type = "repository"
86	        is_peer = bool(
87	            pub_type in ("article", "review") and src_type == "journal"
88	        )
89	    return {
90	        "openalex_publication_type": pub_type,
91	        "openalex_source_type": src_type,
92	        "openalex_is_peer_reviewed": is_peer,
93	        "fetched_content_length": content_len,
94	    }
95	
96	
97	def _authority_payload(signals: dict, row: dict) -> dict:
98	    """Recover the additive AuthoritySignals payload from the recovered signals.
99	
100	    Only fields the audit trail supports are populated; everything else stays
101	    None so the authority model lands honest LOW/medium confidence (the corpus
102	    dumps did not record cited_by_count / venue stats / ROR, so those are
103	    legitimately absent → the model degrades honestly, never fabricates).
104	    """
105	    return {
106	        "cited_by_count": None,
107	        "source_id": "",
108	        "venue_summary_stats": None,
109	        "is_core": None,
110	        "is_in_doaj": None,
111	        "apc_prices": None,
112	        "publication_year": None,
113	        "ror_id": "",
114	        "institution_type": "",
115	        "country_code": "",
116	    }
117	
118	
119	def _recover_title_with_marker(row: dict) -> str:
120	    """Recover the title-derived article-type signal HEAD used.
121	
122	    HEAD classified via the OpenAlex display_name (full title), which the dump
123	    only stored truncated. When HEAD's rule/reason records that the FULL title
124	    signaled SR/MA or narrative review, that marker WAS in the real OpenAlex
125	    title — recover it onto the reconstructed title so the offline replay sees
126	    the same title signal HEAD saw. This recovers the actual recorded signal
127	    (audit trail), it does NOT invent a tier from url+title.
128	    """
129	    rule = row.get("tier_rule", "") or ""
130	    reason = " ".join(row.get("tier_reasons") or [])
131	    title = row.get("title", "") or ""
132	    if rule == "R9_openalex_sr_or_ma" or "title signals systematic review" in reason:
133	        if "systematic review" not in title.lower() and "meta-analysis" not in title.lower():
134	            return (title + " — systematic review and meta-analysis").strip(" —")
135	    if rule == "R9_openalex_narrative_review" or "title signals narrative" in reason:
136	        if "review" not in title.lower():
137	            return (title + " — a narrative review").strip(" —")
138	    return title
139	
140	
141	def main() -> None:
142	    seen: dict[str, dict] = {}
143	    for path in sorted(glob.glob(CLINICAL_GLOB, recursive=True)):
144	        try:
145	            rows = json.loads(Path(path).read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
146	        except (ValueError, OSError):
147	            continue
148	        if not isinstance(rows, list):
149	            continue
150	        for row in rows:
151	            if isinstance(row, dict) and row.get("url"):
152	                seen.setdefault(row["url"], row)
153	
154	    OUT_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
155	    lines: list[str] = []
156	    for url in sorted(seen):
157	        row = seen[url]
158	        signals = _recover_signals(row)
159	        entry = {
160	            "url": url,
161	            "title": _recover_title_with_marker(row),
162	            "domain": row.get("domain", "") or "",
163	            "signals": signals,
164	            "authority_signals": _authority_payload(signals, row),
165	            "head_tier": row.get("tier", ""),
166	            "head_rule": row.get("tier_rule", ""),
167	            # Diff-gate P1-C — structural junk INPUTS are EMPTY for every offline
168	            # row. The frozen corpus dumps recorded ONLY stripped content; they
169	            # never captured the raw ld+json <script> blocks (destroyed by
170	            # _strip_html) nor single-vendor self-interest tokens. So a
171	            # production-realistic OFFLINE replay has none of these signals —
172	            # injecting synthetic JSON-LD / vendor tokens would launder the S2
173	            # number (the iter-2 artifact Codex flagged). These fields fire only
174	            # after the one-time FREE Gate-A live re-fetch (raw HTML → JSON-LD
175	            # via the now-wired live_retriever path; OpenAlex /sources →
176	            # predatory-OA; vendor-token extraction → self-interest). LAW II.
177	            "fetched_body": "",
178	            "structured_jsonld": "",
179	            "claim_vendor_token": "",
180	        }
181	        lines.append(json.dumps(entry, ensure_ascii=False))
182	
183	    OUT_PATH.write_text("\n".join(lines) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
184	    print(f"froze {len(lines)} clinical S2 fixtures -> {OUT_PATH}")
185	
186	
187	if __name__ == "__main__":
188	    main()
189

1	"""Build the S3 cross-field + S4 adversarial-thin authority fixtures (GH #983).
2	
3	These are TEST FIXTURES (allowed synthetic test inputs per LAW VI / §9.4 — they
4	live under tests/fixtures/). Each row is a non-clinical source with a mocked
5	OpenAlex/ROR AuthoritySignals payload + structural fields. The expected
6	source_class / confidence band is asserted in the S3 / S4 tests.
7	
8	S3 cross-field (>=50): law / physics / policy / JP-gov / African-energy with
9	defensible source_class + honest confidence.
10	S4 adversarial-thin: grey-lit / non-English / niche-regional with deliberately
11	THIN OpenAlex -> must land honest-LOW confidence in the mid-band.
12	"""
13	from __future__ import annotations
14	
15	import json
16	from pathlib import Path
17	
18	REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
19	FIX_DIR = REPO_ROOT / "tests" / "fixtures" / "authority"
20	
21	
22	def _row(url, field, signals, structural=None, expect_class=None, expect_conf=None):
23	    return {
24	        "url": url,
25	        "field": field,
26	        "authority_signals": signals,
27	        "structural": structural or {},
28	        "expect_source_class": expect_class,
29	        "expect_confidence": expect_conf,
30	    }
31	
32	
33	def _scholarly(cited, h_index, two_yr, inst_type="education", core=True, doaj=True,
34	               year=2022, country="US"):
35	    return {
36	        "cited_by_count": cited,
37	        "source_id": "https://openalex.org/S100",
38	        "venue_summary_stats": {"h_index": h_index, "2yr_mean_citedness": two_yr},
39	        "is_core": core,
40	        "is_in_doaj": doaj,
41	        "apc_prices": None,
42	        "publication_year": year,
43	        "ror_id": "https://ror.org/edu1",
44	        "institution_type": inst_type,
45	        "country_code": country,
46	    }
47	
48	
49	def _official(inst_type="government", country="JP"):
50	    return {
51	        "cited_by_count": None,
52	        "source_id": "",
53	        "venue_summary_stats": None,
54	        "is_core": None,
55	        "is_in_doaj": None,
56	        "apc_prices": None,
57	        "publication_year": 2023,
58	        "ror_id": "https://ror.org/gov1",
59	        "institution_type": inst_type,
60	        "country_code": country,
61	    }
62	
63	
64	def _thin(year=None):
65	    return {
66	        "cited_by_count": None,
67	        "source_id": "",
68	        "venue_summary_stats": None,
69	        "is_core": None,
70	        "is_in_doaj": None,
71	        "apc_prices": None,
72	        "publication_year": year,
73	        "ror_id": "",
74	        "institution_type": "",
75	        "country_code": "",
76	    }
77	
78	
79	def build_cross_field() -> list[dict]:
80	    rows: list[dict] = []
81	    # Physics journals (PRIMARY_SCHOLARLY).
82	    for i in range(12):
83	        rows.append(_row(
84	            f"https://journals.aps.org/prl/abstract/physics{i}", "physics",
85	            _scholarly(800 + i, 250, 9.0), expect_class="PRIMARY_SCHOLARLY",
86	            expect_conf="HIGH",
87	        ))
88	    # JP government ministries via ROR + PSL gov suffix (PRIMARY_OFFICIAL).
89	    jp_hosts = ["mhlw.go.jp", "meti.go.jp", "mext.go.jp", "env.go.jp",
90	                "soumu.go.jp", "maff.go.jp"]
91	    for h in jp_hosts:
92	        rows.append(_row(
93	            f"https://www.{h}/policy/doc.html", "jp_gov",
94	            _official("government", "JP"), expect_class="PRIMARY_OFFICIAL",
95	            expect_conf="HIGH",
96	        ))
97	    # African-energy policy bodies (PRIMARY_OFFICIAL via ROR government).
98	    for i, h in enumerate(["energy.go.ke", "nersa.org.za", "energycommission.gov.gh"]):
99	        rows.append(_row(
100	            f"https://www.{h}/report{i}", "african_energy",
101	            _official("government", "KE" if i == 0 else "ZA"),
102	            expect_class="PRIMARY_OFFICIAL", expect_conf="HIGH",
103	        ))
104	    # Policy think-tank / nonprofit (SECONDARY).
105	    for i in range(8):
106	        rows.append(_row(
107	            f"https://policyinstitute.test/brief{i}", "policy",
108	            _official("nonprofit", "GB"), expect_class="SECONDARY",
109	            expect_conf="MEDIUM",
110	        ))
111	    # Law-firm commentary blogs (COMMENTARY via junk path /blog/).
112	    for i in range(12):
113	        rows.append(_row(
114	            f"https://lawfirm{i}.test/blog/analysis-{i}", "law",
115	            _thin(2024), structural={"url_path": f"/blog/analysis-{i}"},
116	            expect_class="COMMENTARY", expect_conf=None,
117	        ))
118	    # Law scholarship (education ROR + journal) PRIMARY_SCHOLARLY.
119	    for i in range(10):
120	        rows.append(_row(
121	            f"https://lawreview.test/article/{i}", "law",
122	            _scholarly(40 + i, 60, 3.0, inst_type="education"),
123	            expect_class="PRIMARY_SCHOLARLY", expect_conf="HIGH",
124	        ))
125	    return rows
126	
127	
128	def build_adversarial_thin() -> list[dict]:
129	    rows: list[dict] = []
130	    # Grey-lit / non-English / niche-regional, deliberately THIN OpenAlex.
131	    samples = [
132	        ("https://greylit.test/working-paper-1", "grey_lit"),
133	        ("https://greylit.test/working-paper-2", "grey_lit"),
134	        ("https://revista-regional.test/articulo-1", "non_english"),
135	        ("https://revista-regional.test/articulo-2", "non_english"),
136	        ("https://niche-regional.test/report-a", "niche_regional"),
137	        ("https://niche-regional.test/report-b", "niche_regional"),
138	        ("https://obscure-journal.test/paper-x", "grey_lit"),
139	        ("https://local-ministry-archive.test/doc-7", "niche_regional"),
140	        ("https://small-ngo.test/whitepaper", "grey_lit"),
141	        ("https://regional-univ.test/thesis-3", "non_english"),
142	    ]
143	    for url, field in samples:
144	        rows.append(_row(url, field, _thin(2021), expect_conf="LOW"))
145	    return rows
146	
147	
148	def main() -> None:
149	    FIX_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
150	    cross = build_cross_field()
151	    thin = build_adversarial_thin()
152	    (FIX_DIR / "cross_field_50_urls.jsonl").write_text(
153	        "\n".join(json.dumps(r, ensure_ascii=False) for r in cross) + "\n",
154	        encoding="utf-8",
155	    )
156	    (FIX_DIR / "adversarial_thin_field.jsonl").write_text(
157	        "\n".join(json.dumps(r, ensure_ascii=False) for r in thin) + "\n",
158	        encoding="utf-8",
159	    )
160	    print(f"cross_field rows: {len(cross)}  adversarial_thin rows: {len(thin)}")
161	
162	
163	if __name__ == "__main__":
164	    main()
165

1	"""Build a high-resolution PDF of all POLARIS frontend walkthrough screenshots.
2	
3	Each page = 1 screenshot at full resolution, landscape A3 for maximum clarity.
4	Includes a title page and per-page captions.
5	"""
6	
7	import os
8	import sys
9	from pathlib import Path
10	
11	from fpdf import FPDF
12	from PIL import Image
13	
14	
15	OUTPUT_DIR = Path(os.getenv("POLARIS_OUTPUT_DIR", "outputs"))
16	PDF_PATH = OUTPUT_DIR / "polaris_frontend_walkthrough.pdf"
17	
18	SCREENSHOTS = [
19	    ("walkthrough_01_landing_dark.png", "Page 1: Landing — Report View, Researcher Mode, Dark Theme",
20	     "Default landing state. Shows the POLARIS header bar with role selector (Researcher/Operator), "
21	     "Pipeline Console badge, theme toggle (moon icon), SSE status (Ready), event counter (105 events), "
22	     "cost tracker ($0.000), reconnect counter, tab count, memory status, and Sovereign badge. "
23	     "Below: the research query in a teal banner, 6 navigation tabs (Research, Evidence 126, Report, "
24	     "Memory, Pipelines, Advanced), and the Report view with verification banner, 5-metric stats dashboard, "
25	     "table of contents sidebar, and rendered report body."),
26	
27	    ("walkthrough_02_landing_light.png", "Page 2: Landing — Report View, Researcher Mode, Light Theme",
28	     "Same view after clicking the theme toggle. All colors invert: dark header becomes white, "
29	     "dark body becomes light gray, text flips from white-on-dark to dark-on-light. "
30	     "Demonstrates full theme coverage — every component respects the data-theme attribute."),
31	
32	    ("walkthrough_03_landing_operator.png", "Page 3: Landing — Report View, Operator Mode",
33	     "Same view after switching role to Operator. The role chip changes, and operator-specific "
34	     "UI elements appear. The RBAC system controls which components are visible per role."),
35	
36	    ("walkthrough_04_research.png", "Page 4: Research View",
37	     "The Research tab shows the pipeline execution timeline. Left panel: phase blocks "
38	     "(Plan, Search, STORM Interviews, Analyze, Verify, Evaluate, Synthesize) each expandable "
39	     "with reasoning streams. Right panel: a live reasoning stream showing the AI's chain-of-thought "
40	     "during research. Phase blocks have colored status indicators and expand/collapse headers."),
41	
42	    ("walkthrough_05_evidence.png", "Page 5: Evidence View",
43	     "Two-panel layout. Left: a force-directed evidence relationship graph (D3.js) showing "
44	     "connections between evidence pieces with tier-colored nodes (GOLD/SILVER/BRONZE). "
45	     "Right: scrollable evidence cards with tier badges, source URLs, relevance scores, "
46	     "and verification status. Filter chips at top for tier filtering. Evidence count badge on tab."),
47	
48	    ("walkthrough_06_report.png", "Page 6: Report View (Detail)",
49	     "The main deliverable view. Green verification banner: '92 claims verified from 8 sources — "
50	     "82% verification rate across 8,400 words'. Stats dashboard: 82% Claims Verified, "
51	     "126 Evidence Pieces, 8 Sources Cited, 8,400 Words, 2 Verification Passes. "
52	     "Two-column layout: Table of Contents sidebar (sticky) + rendered report with H1 title, "
53	     "Conflicts(2) badge, numbered sections, and inline [1] citation markers."),
54	
55	    ("walkthrough_07_memory.png", "Page 7: Memory View",
56	     "Tri-level knowledge management interface. Header: GOLD 0 / SILVER 0 / BRONZE 0 tier badges "
57	     "with Online status indicator and Refresh button. Two panels: Knowledge Clusters (left) "
58	     "for domain-grouped memories, and searchable Memory Items list (right). "
59	     "Bottom: collapsible Knowledge Timeline for chronological memory tracking. "
60	     "Empty state shown — populates during pipeline execution."),
61	
62	    ("walkthrough_08_pipelines.png", "Page 8: Pipelines View (DAG Editor)",
63	     "Visual pipeline builder. Left sidebar: 5 pipeline templates (Academic Focus, Compliance Review, "
64	     "Multi-Vector Deep Analysis, Quick Scan, Standard Research) each with description and "
65	     "'Use Template' button. Main canvas: toolbar (Save, Validate, Run, Wizard, +, -, Fit) "
66	     "with empty canvas placeholder. Bottom: '+ New Pipeline' button. "
67	     "Canvas supports drag-and-drop node placement and edge drawing."),
68	
69	    ("walkthrough_09_adv_queries.png", "Page 9: Advanced — Queries Sub-Tab",
70	     "Search strategy inspector. Shows: Search Strategy type (multi_perspective_storm), "
71	     "Key Concepts as teal chips (PFAS, activated carbon, ion exchange, reverse osmosis, "
72	     "nanofiltration, cost analysis), Search Engines horizontal bar chart (Serper 64, "
73	     "OpenAlex 5, Exa 4, S2 3, DuckDuckGo 3), and full list of 12 Planned Queries "
74	     "with actual search strings used by the pipeline."),

76	    ("walkthrough_10_adv_sources.png", "Page 10: Advanced — Sources Sub-Tab",
77	     "Source provenance dashboard. Fetch Pipeline card: 5 SUCCESS / 1 SNIPPET / 2 FAILED. "
78	     "Source Domains list (8): doi.org, epa.gov, waterworld.com, sciencedirect.com, pubs.acs.org, "
79	     "blocked-domain.example.com, awwa.org, nature.com. Perspective Distribution bar chart: "
80	     "technical(3), economic(2), regulatory(2), environmental(2), public_health(2), comparative(1). "
81	     "Missing Perspectives section flags coverage gaps."),

84	     "STORM (Synthesis of Topic Outlines through Retrieval and Multi-perspective questioning) "
85	     "persona interface. 6 AI personas in 3x2 card grid: Dr. Sarah Chen (Environmental Engineering), "
86	     "Michael Torres (Water Utility Finance), Jennifer Walsh (EPA Program Lead), "
87	     "Prof. Raj Patel (Environmental Toxicology), Dr. Lisa Kim (Public Health Epidemiology), "
88	     "Dr. James Wright (Environmental History). Each card: avatar, title, research focus, "
89	     "interview/findings counts. Below: full Interview Transcript with color-coded Q&A exchanges."),
90	
91	    ("walkthrough_12_adv_trace.png", "Page 12: Advanced — Trace Sub-Tab",
92	     "Full JSONL observability timeline. Filter chips: All(105), node(0), llm_call(24), "
93	     "evidence(23), reasoning_capture(11), llm_detail(2), storm_transcript(6), pipeline_start(1), "
94	     "node_start(7), node_end(7), query(1), search_result(12), fetch(8), iteration_decision(1), "
95	     "quality_gate(2). Chronological event stream with timestamps, color-coded event type badges, "
96	     "and graph node labels. Shows full pipeline execution flow from start to finish."),
97	
98	    ("walkthrough_13_adv_cost.png", "Page 13: Advanced — Cost Sub-Tab",
99	     "LLM cost transparency dashboard. Usage Summary 2x2 grid: 24 Total Calls, 93.0K Input Tokens, "
100	     "30.2K Output Tokens, $0.000 Total Cost. Model Distribution: moonshotai/kimi-k2-instruct "
101	     "at 2 calls (100%). Recent LLM Calls table: section_write (4200->2400 tokens), "
102	     "plan:query_generation (1250->2800 tokens). Full per-call cost breakdown for budget tracking."),
103	]
104	
105	
106	def _sanitize(text):
107	    """Replace Unicode chars that latin-1 core fonts cannot render."""
108	    return (text
109	            .replace("\u2014", " - ")   # em-dash
110	            .replace("\u2013", " - ")   # en-dash
111	            .replace("\u2018", "'")     # left single quote
112	            .replace("\u2019", "'")     # right single quote
113	            .replace("\u201c", '"')     # left double quote
114	            .replace("\u201d", '"')     # right double quote
115	            .replace("\u2026", "...")   # ellipsis
116	            .replace("\u2192", "->")   # arrow
117	            )
118	
119	
120	def build_pdf():
121	    """Build landscape PDF with one screenshot per page at maximum resolution."""
122	    pdf = FPDF(orientation="L", unit="mm", format="A3")
123	    pdf.set_auto_page_break(auto=False)
124	
125	    page_w = 420  # A3 landscape width mm
126	    page_h = 297  # A3 landscape height mm
127	    margin = 10
128	
129	    # ── Title Page ──────────────────────────────────────────────
130	    pdf.add_page()
131	    pdf.set_font("Helvetica", "B", 36)
132	    pdf.set_y(60)
133	    pdf.cell(page_w - 2 * margin, 20, _sanitize("POLARIS"), align="C", new_x="LMARGIN", new_y="NEXT")
134	    pdf.set_font("Helvetica", "", 20)
135	    pdf.cell(page_w - 2 * margin, 14, _sanitize("Autonomous Research Pipeline & Intelligence Dashboard"), align="C", new_x="LMARGIN", new_y="NEXT")
136	    pdf.ln(10)
137	    pdf.set_font("Helvetica", "B", 16)
138	    pdf.cell(page_w - 2 * margin, 12, _sanitize("Frontend UI Walkthrough - 13 Pages, All Views & Modes"), align="C", new_x="LMARGIN", new_y="NEXT")
139	    pdf.ln(20)
140	
141	    pdf.set_font("Helvetica", "", 12)
142	    lines = [
143	        "This document contains pixel-perfect screenshots of every view, theme, and mode",
144	        "in the POLARIS frontend dashboard. Each page shows one full-viewport capture at",
145	        "1440x900 resolution (desktop). The screenshots are taken from a live running instance",
146	        "with real mock data injected to show all UI components in their populated states.",
147	        "",
148	        "Views covered: Landing (3 variants), Research, Evidence, Report, Memory,",
149	        "Pipelines, Advanced (Queries, Sources, STORM, Trace, Cost).",
150	        "",
151	        "Prepared for external UI/UX design review.",
152	        f"Total screenshots: {len(SCREENSHOTS)}",
153	    ]
154	    for line in lines:
155	        pdf.cell(page_w - 2 * margin, 8, _sanitize(line), align="C", new_x="LMARGIN", new_y="NEXT")
156	
157	    # ── Screenshot Pages ────────────────────────────────────────
158	    for filename, title, description in SCREENSHOTS:
159	        img_path = OUTPUT_DIR / filename
160	        if not img_path.exists():
161	            print(f"WARNING: Missing {img_path}, skipping")
162	            continue
163	
164	        with Image.open(img_path) as img:
165	            img_w, img_h = img.size
166	
167	        pdf.add_page()
168	
169	        # Title bar
170	        pdf.set_font("Helvetica", "B", 14)
171	        pdf.set_xy(margin, margin)
172	        pdf.cell(page_w - 2 * margin, 8, _sanitize(title), new_x="LMARGIN", new_y="NEXT")
173	
174	        # Image — maximize area below title, above description
175	        title_h = 12
176	        desc_h = 32
177	        avail_w = page_w - 2 * margin
178	        avail_h = page_h - 2 * margin - title_h - desc_h
179	
180	        # Scale image to fit available area while preserving aspect ratio
181	        scale_w = avail_w / (img_w * 0.264583)  # px to mm at 96dpi: 1px = 0.264583mm
182	        scale_h = avail_h / (img_h * 0.264583)
183	        scale = min(scale_w, scale_h, 1.5)  # cap upscale at 1.5x
184	
185	        render_w = img_w * 0.264583 * scale
186	        render_h = img_h * 0.264583 * scale
187	
188	        # Center horizontally
189	        x_offset = margin + (avail_w - render_w) / 2
190	        y_offset = margin + title_h
191	
192	        pdf.image(str(img_path), x=x_offset, y=y_offset, w=render_w, h=render_h)
193	
194	        # Description below image
195	        desc_y = y_offset + render_h + 2
196	        pdf.set_xy(margin, desc_y)
197	        pdf.set_font("Helvetica", "", 9)
198	        pdf.multi_cell(page_w - 2 * margin, 4.5, _sanitize(description))
199	
200	    pdf.output(str(PDF_PATH))
201	    file_size = PDF_PATH.stat().st_size
202	    print(f"PDF saved: {PDF_PATH}")
203	    print(f"Size: {file_size:,} bytes ({file_size / 1024 / 1024:.1f} MB)")
204	    print(f"Pages: {len(SCREENSHOTS) + 1} (1 title + {len(SCREENSHOTS)} screenshots)")
205	
206	
207	if __name__ == "__main__":
208	    build_pdf()
209
