1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""CI gate — block secret-bearing / non-slim .codex/** artifacts (I-sec-001 #535).
3	
4	Invoked by ``.github/workflows/codex_artifact_gate.yml``. That workflow uses
5	``pull_request_target``, so this script runs from the trusted base ref; the
6	PR's own copy is never executed. PR content is inspected as data only.
7	
8	For every changed ``.codex/**`` path in the PR (git status A/M/R/C — deletions
9	are ignored):
10	  * denylist — raw ``codex exec`` transcript filename patterns are rejected
11	    (verdict-only commit policy);
12	  * allowlist — only the slim per-issue artifact filenames are permitted;
13	  * content  — verdict/audit artifacts must validate as schema-bounded slim
14	    blocks via ``extract_codex_verdict.py validate``;
15	  * secret scan — every changed ``.codex/**`` file is scanned with
16	    ``scan_for_secrets.py --strict``.
17	
18	Fast-passes (exit 0) when the PR changes no ``.codex/**`` path — so the gate
19	can be a *required* status check without deadlocking non-.codex PRs.
20	"""
21	from __future__ import annotations
22	
23	import argparse
24	import re
25	import subprocess
26	import sys
27	from pathlib import Path
28	
29	# Raw codex-exec transcripts must never be committed (verdict-only policy).
30	DENY_RE = re.compile(
31	    r"(codex_.*review.*\.txt"
32	    r"|codex_.*audit_iter.*\.txt"
33	    r"|codex_.*_iter[0-9]+\.txt)$",
34	    re.I,
35	)
36	# Slim per-issue artifacts permitted under .codex/<issue_id>/.
37	ALLOWED_BASENAMES = {
38	    "brief.md", "diff_brief.md", "codex_brief_verdict.txt",
39	    "codex_diff_audit.txt", "codex_diff.patch",
40	}
41	FORCE_APPROVE_RE = re.compile(r".*_iter5_force_approve\.txt$")
42	# Artifacts whose content must be a schema-bounded slim verdict.
43	VALIDATE_RE = re.compile(
44	    r"(codex_brief_verdict\.txt|codex_diff_audit\.txt|.*_iter5_force_approve\.txt)$"
45	)
46	
47	
48	def changed_codex_paths(pr_dir: Path, base_branch: str) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
49	    """[(status, path)] for changed .codex/** paths; deletions dropped."""
50	    subprocess.run(
51	        ["git", "-C", str(pr_dir), "fetch", "--no-tags", "--quiet",
52	         "origin", base_branch],
53	        check=False, capture_output=True,
54	    )
55	    r = subprocess.run(
56	        ["git", "-C", str(pr_dir), "diff", "--name-status",
57	         f"origin/{base_branch}...HEAD", "--", ".codex/"],
58	        capture_output=True, text=True, check=True,
59	    )
60	    out: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
61	    for line in r.stdout.splitlines():
62	        parts = line.split("\t")
63	        if len(parts) < 2:
64	            continue
65	        status = parts[0][0]
66	        if status == "D":
67	            continue
68	        out.append((status, parts[-1]))  # dest path (R/C put dest last)
69	    return out
70	
71	
72	def main() -> int:
73	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
74	    ap.add_argument("--pr-checkout", required=True,
75	                    help="dir holding the PR head checkout (data only)")
76	    ap.add_argument("--base-branch", default="polaris")
77	    ap.add_argument("--scanner", required=True,
78	                    help="path to the trusted base scan_for_secrets.py")
79	    ap.add_argument("--validator", required=True,
80	                    help="path to the trusted base extract_codex_verdict.py")
81	    args = ap.parse_args()
82	
83	    pr = Path(args.pr_checkout)
84	    changed = changed_codex_paths(pr, args.base_branch)
85	    if not changed:
86	        print("codex-artifact-gate: no .codex/** changes — PASS (fast)")
87	        return 0
88	
89	    failures: list[str] = []
90	    for status, path in changed:
91	        base = Path(path).name
92	        fpath = pr / path
93	        # `.codex/<issue_id>/<file>` is a per-issue artifact dir (>= 3 path
94	        # parts); `.codex/<file>` is a top-level protocol/checklist doc with
95	        # arbitrary names. The denylist + secret scan apply everywhere; the
96	        # slim-artifact allowlist applies ONLY inside issue dirs (codex diff
97	        # review iter-1 P2 — else AUDIT_CYCLE_PROTOCOL.md etc. would fail).
98	        parts = path.replace("\\", "/").split("/")
99	        in_issue_dir = len(parts) >= 3
100	        if not FORCE_APPROVE_RE.match(base):
101	            if DENY_RE.search(base):
102	                failures.append(
103	                    f"{path}: raw codex exec transcript must not be committed "
104	                    "— commit only the slim verdict (verdict-only policy, "
105	                    "I-sec-001 #535)")
106	                continue
107	            if in_issue_dir and base not in ALLOWED_BASENAMES:
108	                failures.append(
109	                    f"{path}: filename not on the .codex/<id>/ allowlist "
110	                    f"{sorted(ALLOWED_BASENAMES)} (+ *_iter5_force_approve.txt)")
111	                continue
112	        if not fpath.is_file():
113	            continue  # rename edge with content elsewhere — nothing to scan
114	        if VALIDATE_RE.search(base):
115	            v = subprocess.run(
116	                [sys.executable, args.validator, "validate", str(fpath)],
117	                capture_output=True, text=True)
118	            if v.returncode != 0:
119	                failures.append(
120	                    f"{path}: not a schema-bounded slim verdict — "
121	                    f"{(v.stdout + ' ' + v.stderr).strip()}")
122	        s = subprocess.run(
123	            [sys.executable, args.scanner, str(fpath), "--strict"],
124	            capture_output=True, text=True)
125	        if s.returncode != 0:
126	            failures.append(
127	                f"{path}: secret pattern detected by scan_for_secrets")
128	
129	    if failures:
130	        print("codex-artifact-gate: FAIL")
131	        for f in failures:
132	            print(f"  - {f}")
133	        return 1
134	    print(f"codex-artifact-gate: PASS — {len(changed)} .codex/** path(s) clean")
135	    return 0
136	
137	
138	if __name__ == "__main__":
139	    sys.exit(main())
140

content.text: Interesting: `FORCE_APPROVE_RE` and file pattern `*_iter5_force_approve.txt` — "force_approve" is a suspicious sounding mechanism name that could raise eyebrows in external review (looks like a bypass mechanism), but it's a legitimate documented allowlist entry, not marketing/slang. I'll flag it as worth noting given "force" connotation in a security gate context — but per our strict criteria list, "force" isn't in the banned categories. I'll leave it KEEP. Continuing.

content.content:
1	"""
2	Cloud storage provider integrations (Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox).
3	
4	OAuth2-based browsing and file import for NotebookLM-style cloud sources.
5	Providers authenticate via popup OAuth flow, store tokens in a local JSON file,
6	and expose list_folder() / download_file() for the live_server endpoints.
7	
8	Zero new dependencies beyond httpx (already in requirements).
9	"""
10	
11	import hashlib
12	import json
13	import logging
14	import os
15	import secrets
16	import time
17	from pathlib import Path
18	from typing import Any, Optional
19	
20	import httpx
21	from dotenv import load_dotenv
22	
23	load_dotenv()
24	
25	logger = logging.getLogger("cloud_providers")
26	
27	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
28	# Token Store — persists OAuth2 tokens to disk (LAW VI)
29	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
30	_TOKEN_PATH = Path(os.getenv(
31	    "POLARIS_CLOUD_TOKEN_PATH",
32	    "state/cloud_tokens.json",
33	))
34	
35	
36	class CloudTokenStore:
37	    """Read/write OAuth2 tokens to a local JSON file."""
38	
39	    def __init__(self, path: Path = _TOKEN_PATH) -> None:
40	        self._path = path
41	
42	    def _read_all(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
43	        if not self._path.exists():
44	            return {}
45	        try:
46	            return json.loads(self._path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
47	        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
48	            return {}
49	
50	    def _write_all(self, data: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
51	        self._path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
52	        self._path.write_text(
53	            json.dumps(data, indent=2),
54	            encoding="utf-8",
55	        )
56	
57	    def get(self, provider: str) -> Optional[dict[str, Any]]:
58	        return self._read_all().get(provider)
59	
60	    def save(self, provider: str, token_data: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
61	        all_tokens = self._read_all()
62	        all_tokens[provider] = token_data
63	        self._write_all(all_tokens)
64	
65	    def delete(self, provider: str) -> None:
66	        all_tokens = self._read_all()
67	        all_tokens.pop(provider, None)
68	        self._write_all(all_tokens)
69	
70	    def is_connected(self, provider: str) -> bool:
71	        token = self.get(provider)
72	        return token is not None and bool(token.get("access_token"))
73	
74	
75	# Shared instance
76	_token_store = CloudTokenStore()
77	
78	# CSRF state tokens (in-memory, valid for 10 minutes)
79	_pending_states: dict[str, float] = {}
80	
81	
82	def _generate_state() -> str:
83	    """Generate a CSRF state token and store it with expiry."""
84	    state = secrets.token_urlsafe(32)
85	    _pending_states[state] = time.time() + 600  # 10 min
86	    # Prune expired
87	    now = time.time()
88	    expired = [k for k, v in _pending_states.items() if v < now]
89	    for k in expired:
90	        _pending_states.pop(k, None)
91	    return state
92	
93	
94	def _validate_state(state: str) -> bool:
95	    """Validate and consume a CSRF state token."""
96	    expiry = _pending_states.pop(state, None)
97	    if expiry is None:
98	        return False
99	    return time.time() < expiry
100	
101	
102	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
103	# Base CloudProvider
104	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
105	class CloudProvider:
106	    """Abstract base for OAuth2 cloud storage providers."""
107	
108	    PROVIDER_ID: str = ""
109	    AUTH_URL: str = ""
110	    TOKEN_URL: str = ""
111	    SCOPES: str = ""
112	    CLIENT_ID_ENV: str = ""
113	    CLIENT_SECRET_ENV: str = ""
114	
115	    def __init__(self, token_store: CloudTokenStore) -> None:
116	        self._store = token_store
117	
118	    @property
119	    def client_id(self) -> str:
120	        return os.getenv(self.CLIENT_ID_ENV, "")
121	
122	    @property
123	    def client_secret(self) -> str:
124	        return os.getenv(self.CLIENT_SECRET_ENV, "")
125	
126	    @property
127	    def is_configured(self) -> bool:
128	        return bool(self.client_id and self.client_secret)
129	
130	    @property
131	    def is_connected(self) -> bool:
132	        return self._store.is_connected(self.PROVIDER_ID)
133	
134	    def get_authorize_url(self, redirect_uri: str) -> str:
135	        """Build the OAuth2 authorization URL."""
136	        state = _generate_state()
137	        params = {
138	            "client_id": self.client_id,
139	            "redirect_uri": redirect_uri,
140	            "response_type": "code",
141	            "scope": self.SCOPES,
142	            "state": state,
143	            "access_type": "offline",
144	            "prompt": "consent",
145	        }
146	        query = "&".join(f"{k}={httpx.URL('', params={k: v}).params[k]}"
147	                         for k, v in params.items() if v)
148	        return f"{self.AUTH_URL}?{query}"
149	
150	    def exchange_code(self, code: str, redirect_uri: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
151	        """Exchange authorization code for tokens."""
152	        resp = httpx.post(
153	            self.TOKEN_URL,
154	            data={
155	                "client_id": self.client_id,
156	                "client_secret": self.client_secret,
157	                "code": code,
158	                "redirect_uri": redirect_uri,
159	                "grant_type": "authorization_code",
160	            },
161	            timeout=30.0,
162	        )
163	        resp.raise_for_status()
164	        data = resp.json()
165	        token_data = {
166	            "access_token": data.get("access_token", ""),
167	            "refresh_token": data.get("refresh_token", ""),
168	            "expires_at": time.time() + data.get("expires_in", 3600),
169	            "scope": data.get("scope", self.SCOPES),
170	        }
171	        self._store.save(self.PROVIDER_ID, token_data)
172	        return token_data
173	
174	    def _get_access_token(self) -> str:
175	        """Return a valid access token, refreshing if expired."""
176	        token_data = self._store.get(self.PROVIDER_ID)
177	        if not token_data:
178	            raise PermissionError(f"{self.PROVIDER_ID} not connected")
179	        if time.time() > token_data.get("expires_at", 0) - 60:
180	            refresh = token_data.get("refresh_token", "")
181	            if not refresh:
182	                raise PermissionError(f"{self.PROVIDER_ID} token expired, no refresh token")
183	            token_data = self._refresh_token(refresh)
184	        return token_data["access_token"]
185	
186	    def _refresh_token(self, refresh_token: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
187	        """Use refresh_token to get a new access_token."""
188	        resp = httpx.post(
189	            self.TOKEN_URL,
190	            data={
191	                "client_id": self.client_id,
192	                "client_secret": self.client_secret,
193	                "refresh_token": refresh_token,
194	                "grant_type": "refresh_token",
195	            },
196	            timeout=30.0,
197	        )
198	        resp.raise_for_status()
199	        data = resp.json()
200	        token_data = {
201	            "access_token": data.get("access_token", ""),
202	            "refresh_token": data.get("refresh_token", refresh_token),
203	            "expires_at": time.time() + data.get("expires_in", 3600),
204	            "scope": data.get("scope", self.SCOPES),
205	        }
206	        self._store.save(self.PROVIDER_ID, token_data)
207	        return token_data
208	
209	    def _api_get(self, url: str, params: Optional[dict] = None) -> dict[str, Any]:
210	        """Authenticated JSON GET request."""
211	        token = self._get_access_token()
212	        resp = httpx.get(
213	            url,
214	            params=params,
215	            headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"},
216	            timeout=30.0,
217	            follow_redirects=True,
218	        )
219	        resp.raise_for_status()
220	        return resp.json()
221	
222	    def _api_get_bytes(self, url: str, headers: Optional[dict] = None) -> bytes:
223	        """Authenticated binary GET request (file downloads)."""
224	        token = self._get_access_token()
225	        req_headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
226	        if headers:
227	            req_headers.update(headers)
228	        resp = httpx.get(
229	            url,
230	            headers=req_headers,
231	            timeout=120.0,
232	            follow_redirects=True,
233	        )
234	        resp.raise_for_status()
235	        return resp.content
236	
237	    def _api_post(self, url: str, json_data: Optional[dict] = None,
238	                  headers: Optional[dict] = None) -> httpx.Response:
239	        """Authenticated POST request."""
240	        token = self._get_access_token()
241	        req_headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
242	        if headers:
243	            req_headers.update(headers)
244	        resp = httpx.post(
245	            url,
246	            json=json_data,
247	            headers=req_headers,
248	            timeout=120.0,
249	            follow_redirects=True,
250	        )
251	        resp.raise_for_status()
252	        return resp
253	
254	    def list_folder(self, folder_id: Optional[str] = None) -> dict[str, Any]:
255	        """List folder contents. Returns {items: [...], breadcrumb: [...]}."""
256	        raise NotImplementedError
257	
258	    def download_file(self, file_id: str, mime_type: str = "") -> tuple[str, bytes]:
259	        """Download a file. Returns (filename, bytes)."""
260	        raise NotImplementedError
261	
262	    def disconnect(self) -> None:
263	        """Remove stored tokens for this provider."""
264	        self._store.delete(self.PROVIDER_ID)
265	
266	
267	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
268	# Google Drive
269	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
270	class GoogleDriveProvider(CloudProvider):
271	    """Google Drive via Drive API v3."""
272	
273	    PROVIDER_ID = "google_drive"
274	    AUTH_URL = "https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth"
275	    TOKEN_URL = "https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token"
276	    SCOPES = "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.readonly"
277	    CLIENT_ID_ENV = "GOOGLE_DRIVE_CLIENT_ID"
278	    CLIENT_SECRET_ENV = "GOOGLE_DRIVE_CLIENT_SECRET"
279	
280	    _DRIVE_API = "https://www.googleapis.com/drive/v3"
281	
282	    # Google Workspace MIME types → export targets
283	    _EXPORT_MIMES: dict[str, str] = {
284	        "application/vnd.google-apps.document": "text/plain",
285	        "application/vnd.google-apps.spreadsheet": "text/csv",
286	        "application/vnd.google-apps.presentation": "text/plain",
287	    }
288	
289	    def list_folder(self, folder_id: Optional[str] = None) -> dict[str, Any]:
290	        fid = folder_id or "root"
291	        query = f"'{fid}' in parents and trashed = false"
292	        fields = "files(id,name,mimeType,size,modifiedTime,parents)"
293	        data = self._api_get(
294	            f"{self._DRIVE_API}/files",
295	            params={"q": query, "fields": fields, "pageSize": "100",
296	                    "orderBy": "folder,name"},
297	        )
298	        items = []
299	        for f in data.get("files", []):
300	            is_folder = f.get("mimeType") == "application/vnd.google-apps.folder"
301	            items.append({
302	                "id": f["id"],
303	                "name": f["name"],
304	                "is_folder": is_folder,
305	                "size": int(f.get("size", 0)) if not is_folder else 0,
306	                "mime_type": f.get("mimeType", ""),
307	                "modified": f.get("modifiedTime", ""),
308	            })
309	        breadcrumb = self._build_breadcrumb(fid)
310	        return {"items": items, "breadcrumb": breadcrumb}
311	
312	    def _build_breadcrumb(self, folder_id: str) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
313	        """Walk parent chain to build folder path."""
314	        crumbs: list[dict[str, str]] = []
315	        current = folder_id
316	        seen: set[str] = set()
317	        while current and current != "root" and current not in seen:
318	            seen.add(current)
319	            try:
320	                meta = self._api_get(
321	                    f"{self._DRIVE_API}/files/{current}",
322	                    params={"fields": "id,name,parents"},
323	                )
324	                crumbs.insert(0, {"id": meta["id"], "name": meta.get("name", "")})
325	                parents = meta.get("parents", [])
326	                current = parents[0] if parents else None
327	            except Exception:
328	                break
329	        crumbs.insert(0, {"id": "root", "name": "My Drive"})
330	        return crumbs
331	
332	    def download_file(self, file_id: str, mime_type: str = "") -> tuple[str, bytes]:
333	        # Get file metadata
334	        meta = self._api_get(
335	            f"{self._DRIVE_API}/files/{file_id}",
336	            params={"fields": "id,name,mimeType,size"},
337	        )
338	        name = meta.get("name", file_id)
339	        file_mime = meta.get("mimeType", mime_type)
340	
341	        # Google Workspace docs: export
342	        export_mime = self._EXPORT_MIMES.get(file_mime)
343	        if export_mime:
344	            content = self._api_get_bytes(
345	                f"{self._DRIVE_API}/files/{file_id}/export",
346	                headers={"Accept": export_mime},
347	            )
348	            # Add appropriate extension
349	            if export_mime == "text/plain" and not name.endswith(".txt"):
350	                name += ".txt"
351	            elif export_mime == "text/csv" and not name.endswith(".csv"):
352	                name += ".csv"
353	            return name, content
354	
355	        # Regular files: direct download
356	        content = self._api_get_bytes(
357	            f"{self._DRIVE_API}/files/{file_id}?alt=media",
358	        )
359	        return name, content
360	
361	
362	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
363	# OneDrive (Microsoft Graph)
364	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
365	class OneDriveProvider(CloudProvider):
366	    """OneDrive via Microsoft Graph API."""
367	
368	    PROVIDER_ID = "onedrive"
369	    AUTH_URL = "https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/v2.0/authorize"
370	    TOKEN_URL = "https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/v2.0/token"
371	    SCOPES = "Files.Read offline_access"
372	    CLIENT_ID_ENV = "ONEDRIVE_CLIENT_ID"
373	    CLIENT_SECRET_ENV = "ONEDRIVE_CLIENT_SECRET"
374	
375	    _GRAPH_API = "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0"
376	
377	    def list_folder(self, folder_id: Optional[str] = None) -> dict[str, Any]:
378	        if folder_id and folder_id != "root":
379	            url = f"{self._GRAPH_API}/me/drive/items/{folder_id}/children"
380	        else:
381	            url = f"{self._GRAPH_API}/me/drive/root/children"
382	            folder_id = "root"
383	
384	        data = self._api_get(url, params={
385	            "$select": "id,name,size,lastModifiedDateTime,folder,file",
386	            "$top": "100",
387	            "$orderby": "name",
388	        })
389	
390	        items = []
391	        for item in data.get("value", []):
392	            is_folder = "folder" in item
393	            items.append({
394	                "id": item["id"],
395	                "name": item["name"],
396	                "is_folder": is_folder,
397	                "size": item.get("size", 0) if not is_folder else 0,
398	                "mime_type": item.get("file", {}).get("mimeType", ""),
399	                "modified": item.get("lastModifiedDateTime", ""),
400	            })
401	        breadcrumb = self._build_breadcrumb(folder_id)
402	        return {"items": items, "breadcrumb": breadcrumb}
403	
404	    def _build_breadcrumb(self, folder_id: str) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
405	        crumbs = [{"id": "root", "name": "OneDrive"}]
406	        if folder_id and folder_id != "root":
407	            try:
408	                meta = self._api_get(
409	                    f"{self._GRAPH_API}/me/drive/items/{folder_id}",
410	                    params={"$select": "id,name,parentReference"},
411	                )
412	                # Build path from parentReference
413	                parent_path = meta.get("parentReference", {}).get("path", "")
414	                if parent_path:
415	                    # Path looks like /drive/root:/folder1/folder2
416	                    parts = parent_path.split("root:")[-1].strip("/").split("/")
417	                    for part in parts:
418	                        if part:
419	                            crumbs.append({"id": "", "name": part})
420	                crumbs.append({"id": meta["id"], "name": meta.get("name", "")})
421	            except Exception:
422	                pass
423	        return crumbs
424	
425	    def download_file(self, file_id: str, mime_type: str = "") -> tuple[str, bytes]:
426	        # Get metadata
427	        meta = self._api_get(
428	            f"{self._GRAPH_API}/me/drive/items/{file_id}",
429	            params={"$select": "id,name,size,@microsoft.graph.downloadUrl"},
430	        )
431	        name = meta.get("name", file_id)
432	        download_url = meta.get("@microsoft.graph.downloadUrl", "")
433	
434	        if download_url:
435	            # Direct download (no auth needed for this pre-signed URL)
436	            resp = httpx.get(download_url, timeout=120.0, follow_redirects=True)
437	            resp.raise_for_status()
438	            return name, resp.content
439	
440	        # Fallback: use content endpoint
441	        content = self._api_get_bytes(
442	            f"{self._GRAPH_API}/me/drive/items/{file_id}/content",
443	        )
444	        return name, content
445	
446	
447	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
448	# Dropbox
449	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
450	class DropboxProvider(CloudProvider):
451	    """Dropbox via Dropbox API v2."""
452	
453	    PROVIDER_ID = "dropbox"
454	    AUTH_URL = "https://www.dropbox.com/oauth2/authorize"
455	    TOKEN_URL = "https://api.dropboxapi.com/oauth2/token"
456	    SCOPES = ""  # Dropbox uses app permissions, not scopes
457	    CLIENT_ID_ENV = "DROPBOX_CLIENT_ID"
458	    CLIENT_SECRET_ENV = "DROPBOX_CLIENT_SECRET"
459	
460	    def get_authorize_url(self, redirect_uri: str) -> str:
461	        """Dropbox uses token_access_type instead of access_type."""
462	        state = _generate_state()
463	        params = {
464	            "client_id": self.client_id,
465	            "redirect_uri": redirect_uri,
466	            "response_type": "code",
467	            "state": state,
468	            "token_access_type": "offline",
469	        }
470	        query = "&".join(f"{k}={httpx.URL('', params={k: v}).params[k]}"
471	                         for k, v in params.items() if v)
472	        return f"{self.AUTH_URL}?{query}"
473	
474	    def list_folder(self, folder_id: Optional[str] = None) -> dict[str, Any]:
475	        path = folder_id if folder_id else ""
476	        resp = self._api_post(
477	            "https://api.dropboxapi.com/2/files/list_folder",
478	            json_data={"path": path, "limit": 100},
479	        )
480	        data = resp.json()
481	
482	        items = []
483	        for entry in data.get("entries", []):
484	            is_folder = entry.get(".tag") == "folder"
485	            items.append({
486	                "id": entry.get("path_lower", entry.get("id", "")),
487	                "name": entry.get("name", ""),
488	                "is_folder": is_folder,
489	                "size": entry.get("size", 0) if not is_folder else 0,
490	                "mime_type": "",
491	                "modified": entry.get("server_modified", ""),
492	            })
493	
494	        # Build breadcrumb from path
495	        breadcrumb = [{"id": "", "name": "Dropbox"}]
496	        if path:
497	            parts = path.strip("/").split("/")
498	            current = ""
499	            for part in parts:
500	                current += f"/{part}"
501	                breadcrumb.append({"id": current, "name": part})
502	
503	        return {"items": items, "breadcrumb": breadcrumb}
504	
505	    def download_file(self, file_id: str, mime_type: str = "") -> tuple[str, bytes]:
506	        token = self._get_access_token()
507	        api_arg = json.dumps({"path": file_id})
508	        resp = httpx.post(
509	            "https://content.dropboxapi.com/2/files/download",
510	            headers={
511	                "Authorization": f"Bearer {token}",
512	                "Dropbox-API-Arg": api_arg,
513	            },
514	            timeout=120.0,
515	        )
516	        resp.raise_for_status()
517	        # Filename from Dropbox-API-Result header or path
518	        name = file_id.rsplit("/", 1)[-1] if "/" in file_id else file_id
519	        try:
520	            result_header = resp.headers.get("dropbox-api-result", "{}")
521	            result = json.loads(result_header)
522	            name = result.get("name", name)
523	        except (json.JSONDecodeError, AttributeError):
524	            pass
525	        return name, resp.content
526	
527	
528	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
529	# Provider Registry
530	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
531	cloud_provider_registry: dict[str, CloudProvider] = {
532	    "google_drive": GoogleDriveProvider(_token_store),
533	    "onedrive": OneDriveProvider(_token_store),
534	    "dropbox": DropboxProvider(_token_store),
535	}
536	
537	
538	def validate_state(state: str) -> bool:
539	    """Validate a CSRF state token (exposed for use by live_server)."""
540	    return _validate_state(state)
541	
542	
543	def get_cloud_status() -> dict[str, dict[str, bool]]:
544	    """Return configured/connected status for all providers."""
545	    status = {}
546	    for provider_id, provider in cloud_provider_registry.items():
547	        status[provider_id] = {
548	            "configured": provider.is_configured,
549	            "connected": provider.is_connected,
550	        }
551	    return status
552

1	"""
2	Parse Codex findings frontmatter into loop_state.json-ready dict.
3	
4	Expects findings.md to START with a YAML frontmatter block like:
5	
6	    ---
7	    verdict: READY | NOT_READY | CONDITIONAL
8	    blocker_count: 0
9	    medium_count: 2
10	    rationale: |
11	      Free-form rationale (may span lines).
12	    ---
13	
14	Emits JSON to stdout (or to a specified path). Never raises — always
15	emits a dict, even on parse failure, so the loop orchestrator can
16	decide what to do with malformed Codex output.
17	
18	Usage:
19	    python scripts/codex_loop_parse.py .codex/round_3/findings.md
20	    python scripts/codex_loop_parse.py .codex/round_3/findings.md --out .codex/round_3/verdict.json
21	"""
22	from __future__ import annotations
23	
24	import argparse
25	import json
26	import re
27	import sys
28	from pathlib import Path
29	
30	
31	_FRONTMATTER_RE = re.compile(
32	    r"^\s*---\s*\n(.*?)\n\s*---\s*(?:\n|$)",
33	    re.DOTALL,
34	)
35	
36	
37	def _yaml_parse_minimal(text: str) -> dict:
38	    """Very small YAML-like parser: top-level key:value pairs + block
39	    scalars (`key: |`). Avoids a PyYAML dependency in the loop harness.
40	    """
41	    result: dict = {}
42	    lines = text.splitlines()
43	    i = 0
44	    while i < len(lines):
45	        line = lines[i]
46	        if not line.strip():
47	            i += 1
48	            continue
49	        m = re.match(r"^(\w[\w_]*)\s*:\s*(\|)?\s*(.*)$", line)
50	        if not m:
51	            i += 1
52	            continue
53	        key, block_marker, rest = m.group(1), m.group(2), m.group(3)
54	        if block_marker == "|":
55	            # Block scalar: subsequent indented lines
56	            buf: list[str] = []
57	            i += 1
58	            while i < len(lines):
59	                nxt = lines[i]
60	                if nxt.startswith(" ") or not nxt.strip():
61	                    buf.append(nxt.lstrip())
62	                    i += 1
63	                else:
64	                    break
65	            result[key] = "\n".join(buf).strip()
66	            continue
67	        result[key] = rest.strip().strip("'\"")
68	        i += 1
69	    return result
70	
71	
72	def parse_findings(path: Path) -> dict:
73	    out: dict = {
74	        "verdict": "UNKNOWN",
75	        "blocker_count": None,
76	        "medium_count": None,
77	        "rationale": "",
78	        "parse_ok": False,
79	        "error": "",
80	        "path": str(path),
81	    }
82	    try:
83	        text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
84	    except FileNotFoundError:
85	        out["error"] = f"not_found:{path}"
86	        return out
87	    except Exception as exc:
88	        out["error"] = f"read_error:{exc}"
89	        return out
90	
91	    m = _FRONTMATTER_RE.match(text)
92	    if not m:
93	        out["error"] = "no_frontmatter"
94	        # Fallback: try to find `verdict:` anywhere near the top
95	        first_200 = text[:2000]
96	        fallback_m = re.search(
97	            r"verdict\s*:\s*(READY|NOT_READY|CONDITIONAL|UNKNOWN)",
98	            first_200, re.IGNORECASE,
99	        )
100	        if fallback_m:
101	            out["verdict"] = fallback_m.group(1).upper()
102	        return out
103	
104	    fm_text = m.group(1)
105	    parsed = _yaml_parse_minimal(fm_text)
106	
107	    verdict_raw = (parsed.get("verdict") or "").strip().upper()
108	    if verdict_raw in {"READY", "NOT_READY", "CONDITIONAL"}:
109	        out["verdict"] = verdict_raw
110	    else:
111	        out["verdict"] = "UNKNOWN"
112	        out["error"] = f"unexpected_verdict:{verdict_raw!r}"
113	
114	    for int_key in ("blocker_count", "medium_count"):
115	        raw = parsed.get(int_key, "")
116	        try:
117	            out[int_key] = int(str(raw).strip())
118	        except Exception:
119	            out[int_key] = None
120	
121	    out["rationale"] = parsed.get("rationale", "") or ""
122	    out["parse_ok"] = bool(
123	        out["verdict"] in {"READY", "NOT_READY", "CONDITIONAL"}
124	        and out["blocker_count"] is not None
125	        and out["medium_count"] is not None
126	    )
127	    return out
128	
129	
130	def main() -> int:
131	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
132	    ap.add_argument("findings_path")
133	    ap.add_argument("--out", default="-")
134	    args = ap.parse_args()
135	
136	    result = parse_findings(Path(args.findings_path))
137	    payload = json.dumps(result, indent=2, sort_keys=True) + "\n"
138	    if args.out == "-":
139	        sys.stdout.write(payload)
140	    else:
141	        Path(args.out).parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
142	        Path(args.out).write_text(payload, encoding="utf-8")
143	    return 0
144	
145	
146	if __name__ == "__main__":
147	    sys.exit(main())
148

1	"""
2	Live honest-rebuild vs pre-rebuild PG_LB_SA_02 — real quality spot-check.
3	
4	Produces loopback/audit/_live_vs_prerebuild_spotcheck.md comparing:
5	  1. Corpus composition (pre-rebuild GOLD/SILVER/BRONZE vs
6	     honest-rebuild T1-T7).
7	  2. Where the same URLs appear in both, whether tiers agree.
8	  3. Text-level differences: word count, citation count,
9	     self-graded faithfulness vs external evaluator judgments.
10	  4. A specific audit of the pre-rebuild corpus:
11	     - How many pre-rebuild "GOLD" sources would re-tier to T5/T6/T7
12	       under the honest-rebuild classifier?
13	"""
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import json
17	import sys
18	from pathlib import Path
19	from urllib.parse import urlparse
20	
21	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
22	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))
23	
24	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.tier_classifier import (  # noqa: E402
25	    ClassificationSignals,
26	    classify_source_tier,
27	)
28	
29	
30	def _domain(url: str) -> str:
31	    try:
32	        return (urlparse(url).netloc or "").lower().removeprefix("www.")  # FX-13 (#1125): not lstrip (char-set bug)
33	    except Exception:
34	        return ""
35	
36	
37	def main() -> int:
38	    # ── Load pre-rebuild run ──────────────────────────────────────────
39	    pre_path = ROOT / "outputs/polaris_graph/PG_LB_SA_02.json"
40	    with pre_path.open(encoding="utf-8") as f:
41	        pre = json.load(f)
42	
43	    pre_biblio = pre.get("bibliography", [])
44	    pre_qm = pre.get("quality_metrics", {})
45	    pre_tier_counts: dict[str, int] = {}
46	    for b in pre_biblio:
47	        t = b.get("authority_tier", "?")
48	        pre_tier_counts[t] = pre_tier_counts.get(t, 0) + 1
49	
50	    # ── Re-classify pre-rebuild URLs with honest-rebuild classifier ───
51	    # For each URL in the pre-rebuild bibliography, pull the canonical
52	    # OpenAlex metadata we already stored, build ClassificationSignals,
53	    # and get the T1-T7 tier. This shows how many pre-rebuild GOLDs
54	    # would have been tiered differently by the new rules.
55	    pre_retier: list[dict] = []
56	    retier_counts: dict[str, int] = {}
57	    retier_from_gold: dict[str, int] = {}
58	    for b in pre_biblio:
59	        url = b.get("url", "") or ""
60	        title = b.get("title", "") or ""
61	        # Pre-rebuild stored openalex fields directly on the biblio entry
62	        pub_type = b.get("openalex_publication_type", "") or b.get("publication_type", "")
63	        source_type = b.get("openalex_source_type", "") or b.get("source_type_normalized", "")
64	        content_len = b.get("content_length", 0) or 0
65	        is_peer = bool(
66	            pub_type in ("article", "review") and source_type == "journal"
67	        )
68	
69	        signals = ClassificationSignals(
70	            url=url,
71	            title=title,
72	            publisher=b.get("publisher", "") or "",
73	            fetched_content_length=content_len,
74	            openalex_publication_type=pub_type,
75	            openalex_source_type=source_type,
76	            openalex_is_peer_reviewed=is_peer,
77	            source_type_hint=b.get("source_type", "") or "",
78	        )
79	        r = classify_source_tier(signals)
80	        new_tier = r.tier.value
81	        retier_counts[new_tier] = retier_counts.get(new_tier, 0) + 1
82	        old_tier = b.get("authority_tier", "?")
83	        if old_tier == "GOLD":
84	            retier_from_gold[new_tier] = retier_from_gold.get(new_tier, 0) + 1
85	
86	        pre_retier.append({
87	            "url": url,
88	            "domain": _domain(url),
89	            "title": title[:80],
90	            "old_tier": old_tier,
91	            "new_tier": new_tier,
92	            "new_rule": r.matched_rules[0] if r.matched_rules else "",
93	        })
94	
95	    # ── Load live honest-rebuild run ──────────────────────────────────
96	    live_dir = ROOT / "outputs/honest_live_cycle"
97	    with (live_dir / "live_corpus_dump.json").open(encoding="utf-8") as f:
98	        live_corpus = json.load(f)
99	    live_tier_counts: dict[str, int] = {}
100	    for s in live_corpus:
101	        t = s.get("tier", "?")
102	        live_tier_counts[t] = live_tier_counts.get(t, 0) + 1
103	
104	    with (live_dir / "manifest.json").open(encoding="utf-8") as f:
105	        live_manifest = json.load(f)
106	    _judge_artifact = live_dir / "judge_output.json"
107	    if not _judge_artifact.exists():
108	        _judge_artifact = live_dir / "qwen_judge_output.json"  # I-modref-004 (#530) legacy
109	    with _judge_artifact.open(encoding="utf-8") as f:
110	        judge = json.load(f)
111	    with (live_dir / "evaluator_rule_checks.json").open(encoding="utf-8") as f:
112	        rule_checks = json.load(f)
113	    with (live_dir / "contradictions.json").open(encoding="utf-8") as f:
114	        contradictions = json.load(f)
115	
116	    # ── Overlap: URLs present in both corpora ────────────────────────
117	    pre_urls = {b.get("url", "") for b in pre_biblio}
118	    live_urls = {s.get("url", "") for s in live_corpus}
119	    overlap = pre_urls & live_urls
120	
121	    # ── Render comparison report ──────────────────────────────────────
122	    out_path = ROOT / "loopback/audit/_live_vs_prerebuild_spotcheck.md"
123	    lines: list[str] = []
124	    lines.append("# Live honest-rebuild vs pre-rebuild PG_LB_SA_02 — spot-check")
125	    lines.append("")
126	    lines.append("_Auto-generated by `scripts/compare_live_vs_pg_lb_sa_02.py`._")
127	    lines.append("")
128	    lines.append("## Queries compared")
129	    lines.append("")
130	    lines.append(f"- **Pre-rebuild** (`PG_LB_SA_02.json`): {pre.get('original_query', '')!r}")
131	    lines.append(f"- **Live honest-rebuild**: {live_manifest.get('research_question', '')!r}")
132	    lines.append("")
133	    lines.append("Same topic (semaglutide for adults with obesity).")
134	    lines.append("")
135	
136	    # Section 1
137	    lines.append("## 1. Corpus-size + tier distribution")
138	    lines.append("")
139	    lines.append("| Metric | Pre-rebuild | Live honest-rebuild |")
140	    lines.append("|---|---|---|")
141	    lines.append(f"| Unique sources | {pre_qm.get('unique_sources', len(pre_biblio))} | {len(live_corpus)} |")
142	    lines.append(f"| Evidence rows  | {pre_qm.get('total_evidence', '?')} | {len(live_manifest.get('generator', {}))} |")
143	    lines.append(f"| Total words    | {pre_qm.get('total_words', '?')} | — (live run is one paragraph) |")
144	    lines.append(f"| Total citations | {pre_qm.get('total_citations', '?')} | {live_manifest.get('verifier', {}).get('verified', '?')} (1 per verified sentence) |")
145	    lines.append(f"| Self-graded faithfulness | **{pre_qm.get('faithfulness_score', 0)*100:.1f}%** (survivorship-biased) | REMOVED (see below) |")
146	    lines.append("")
147	    lines.append("Tier distribution:")
148	    lines.append("")
149	    lines.append("| Pre-rebuild GOLD/SILVER/BRONZE | Count | ... | Live T1-T7 | Count |")
150	    lines.append("|---|---|---|---|---|")
151	    pre_order = ["GOLD", "SILVER", "BRONZE", "UNKNOWN", "BLOCKED"]
152	    live_order = ["T1", "T2", "T3", "T4", "T5", "T6", "T7", "UNKNOWN"]
153	    for i in range(max(len(pre_order), len(live_order))):
154	        pre_t = pre_order[i] if i < len(pre_order) else ""
155	        live_t = live_order[i] if i < len(live_order) else ""
156	        pre_c = pre_tier_counts.get(pre_t, "") if pre_t else ""
157	        live_c = live_tier_counts.get(live_t, "") if live_t else ""
158	        lines.append(f"| {pre_t} | {pre_c} |  | {live_t} | {live_c} |")
159	    lines.append("")
160	
161	    # Section 2
162	    lines.append("## 2. Pre-rebuild bibliography re-classified under honest-rebuild rules")
163	    lines.append("")
164	    lines.append(f"The Phase-2a tier classifier was run against all {len(pre_biblio)} pre-rebuild sources.")
165	    lines.append("")
166	    lines.append("Re-tier counts:")
167	    lines.append("")
168	    for k in sorted(retier_counts.keys()):
169	        lines.append(f"- {k}: {retier_counts[k]}")
170	    lines.append("")
171	    lines.append(f"Pre-rebuild GOLD sources re-tiered to:")
172	    lines.append("")
173	    for k in sorted(retier_from_gold.keys()):
174	        lines.append(f"- {k}: {retier_from_gold[k]}")
175	    lines.append("")
176	    lines.append("### Per-source detail (GOLD → new tier disagreements)")
177	    lines.append("")
178	    lines.append("| URL | Pre-rebuild | Honest-rebuild | Rule | Title |")
179	    lines.append("|---|---|---|---|---|")
180	    for row in pre_retier:
181	        if row["old_tier"] != row["new_tier"]:
182	            short_url = row["url"][:50] + "…" if len(row["url"]) > 50 else row["url"]
183	            lines.append(
184	                f"| {short_url} | {row['old_tier']} | {row['new_tier']} | "
185	                f"`{row['new_rule']}` | {row['title']} |"
186	            )
187	    lines.append("")
188	
189	    # Section 3
190	    lines.append("## 3. URL overlap between the two runs")
191	    lines.append("")
192	    lines.append(f"- URLs in pre-rebuild only: {len(pre_urls - live_urls)}")
193	    lines.append(f"- URLs in live only: {len(live_urls - pre_urls)}")
194	    lines.append(f"- URLs in both: {len(overlap)}")
195	    if overlap:
196	        lines.append("")
197	        lines.append("Overlap URLs:")
198	        lines.append("")
199	        for url in sorted(overlap):
200	            # Find tier assignments
201	            pre_tier = next(
202	                (b.get("authority_tier", "?") for b in pre_biblio
203	                 if b.get("url") == url),
204	                "?",
205	            )
206	            live_tier = next(
207	                (s.get("tier", "?") for s in live_corpus
208	                 if s.get("url") == url),
209	                "?",
210	            )
211	            lines.append(f"- `{url}` — pre={pre_tier}, live={live_tier}")
212	    lines.append("")
213	
214	    # Section 4
215	    lines.append("## 4. Evaluator output — pre-rebuild vs live")
216	    lines.append("")
217	    lines.append("**Pre-rebuild** reported a single `faithfulness_score` of "
218	                 f"**{pre_qm.get('faithfulness_score', 0)*100:.1f}%**. That metric was survivorship-biased "
219	                 "(FIX-QM7 filtered unfaithful evidence, FIX-043A recomputed on "
220	                 "survivors) and is no longer emitted by the honest-rebuild "
221	                 "pipeline. See Phase 1a.")
222	    lines.append("")
223	    lines.append("**Live honest-rebuild** emits:")
224	    lines.append("")
225	    pass_count = sum(1 for r in rule_checks.get("rule_checks", []) if r.get("passed"))
226	    total = len(rule_checks.get("rule_checks", []))
227	    lines.append(f"- Rule-based PRISMA-trAIce: {pass_count}/{total} pass")
228	    for r in rule_checks.get("rule_checks", []):
229	        if not r.get("passed"):
230	            lines.append(f"  - FAIL {r['item_id']} — {r.get('details', '')[:150]}")
231	    lines.append("")
232	    lines.append(f"- Non-same-family judge (model={judge.get('model')}, "
233	                 f"parse_ok={judge.get('parse_ok')}):")
234	    if judge.get("parse_ok"):
235	        for axis, v in judge.get("verdicts", {}).items():
236	            lines.append(f"  - **{v['verdict'].upper()}** {axis}: {v['note']}")
237	    lines.append("")
238	
239	    # Section 5
240	    lines.append("## 5. Contradiction detector — live findings")
241	    lines.append("")
242	    if not contradictions:
243	        lines.append("No contradictions detected.")
244	    else:
245	        for c in contradictions:
246	            vals = ", ".join(str(cc["value"]) for cc in c.get("claims", []))
247	            lines.append(
248	                f"- **{c['subject']} / {c['predicate']}** — values [{vals}] "
249	                f"{c['claims'][0]['unit']}, rel diff "
250	                f"{c['relative_difference']*100:.1f}%, severity={c['severity']}."
251	            )
252	    lines.append("")
253	    lines.append("The pre-rebuild run had no contradiction detector, so no "
254	                 "equivalent disclosure exists there. Any numeric disagreements "
255	                 "in the pre-rebuild corpus were silently reconciled by the "
256	                 "generator.")
257	    lines.append("")
258	
259	    # Section 6
260	    lines.append("## 6. Honest summary — what changed materially")
261	    lines.append("")
262	    lines.append("**Real improvements the live run demonstrates:**")
263	    lines.append("")
264	    lines.append(f"1. Corpus tier is now visible AND non-cooked. Pre-rebuild "
265	                 f"reported {pre_tier_counts.get('GOLD', 0)}/{len(pre_biblio)} = "
266	                 f"{pre_tier_counts.get('GOLD', 0)*100/len(pre_biblio):.0f}% GOLD. "
267	                 f"Re-tiered with honest-rebuild rules, those same URLs split "
268	                 f"across {len([k for k in retier_counts if retier_counts[k] > 0])} "
269	                 f"distinct tiers.")
270	    lines.append("")
271	    pre_gold_still_t1 = retier_from_gold.get("T1", 0)
272	    pre_gold_total = pre_tier_counts.get("GOLD", 0)
273	    if pre_gold_total > 0:
274	        pct_still_t1 = pre_gold_still_t1 / pre_gold_total * 100
275	        lines.append(
276	            f"2. Of the {pre_gold_total} pre-rebuild GOLD sources, "
277	            f"**only {pre_gold_still_t1} ({pct_still_t1:.0f}%) would be T1** "
278	            f"(peer-reviewed primary) under honest-rebuild rules. The rest "
279	            f"were narrative reviews, regulatory docs, industry marketing, "
280	            f"or commentary — all mis-labeled GOLD in the pre-rebuild schema."
281	        )
282	    lines.append("")
283	    lines.append("3. A non-same-family judge produced **specific "
284	                 "per-axis verdicts with notes**. Two axes flagged "
285	                 "`needs_revision` with actionable feedback. This replaces "
286	                 "the pre-rebuild's single self-graded 90.8% number.")
287	    lines.append("")
288	    lines.append("4. Pre-registered `protocol.json` is hashed and tamper-"
289	                 "evident. Pre-rebuild runs had no protocol artifact.")
290	    lines.append("")
291	    lines.append("5. Contradiction detector surfaced a numeric disagreement "
292	                 "(albeit noisily — three heterogeneous percentages were "
293	                 "grouped; see Section 5). The pre-rebuild had no such gate.")
294	    lines.append("")
295	    lines.append("**Honest limitations of the live run:**")
296	    lines.append("")
297	    lines.append("1. Live report is short (371 words vs pre-rebuild's 10,485). "
298	                 "This is by design — strict_verify dropped 2/8 sentences, and "
299	                 "the generator was told to keep it tight. Real usage would "
300	                 "likely relax both constraints.")
301	    lines.append("")
302	    lines.append("2. Contradiction detector picked heterogeneous decimals "
303	                 "(5.0 from a STEP 5 abstract, 6.5 from a press release, "
304	                 "20.7 from a different-dose STEP UP trial). The detector is "
305	                 "too loose — it groups by (subject, predicate, unit) but "
306	                 "doesn't check that the numbers are measuring the same "
307	                 "endpoint or dose. This is a real tuning opportunity.")
308	    lines.append("")
309	    lines.append("3. DeepSeek V3.2 skipped citation markers on the opening "
310	                 "topic sentence and on STEP 5's −15.2% result — strict_verify "
311	                 "correctly dropped both. The dropped STEP 5 sentence quoted "
312	                 "a precise number (−15.2%) that would be a real loss in a "
313	                 "production run.")
314	    lines.append("")
315	    lines.append("4. OpenAlex mis-classified at least two sources: "
316	                 "`novonordiskmedical.com` (Novo-hosted trial PDF) as T1 "
317	                 "peer-reviewed primary, and `prnewswire.com` (Novo press "
318	                 "release) as T4 review. These are both industry voices the "
319	                 "honest-rebuild classifier should demote; fixing requires "
320	                 "hostname-denylist rules that fire before OpenAlex "
321	                 "enrichment.")
322	    lines.append("")
323	
324	    out_path.write_text("\n".join(lines) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
325	    print(f"Wrote {out_path}")
326	    print()
327	    print("Summary:")
328	    print(f"  Pre-rebuild: {pre_tier_counts}")
329	    print(f"  Live:        {live_tier_counts}")
330	    print(f"  Overlap URLs: {len(overlap)}")
331	    if pre_gold_total > 0:
332	        print(f"  Pre-rebuild GOLDs re-tier: {retier_from_gold}")
333	    return 0
334	
335	
336	if __name__ == "__main__":
337	    sys.exit(main())
